target.title: feat(gateway): wire read coding tool into HTTP /tools/invoke (narrow)
target.body:
Related to #66561 (orthogonal — direct-invoke read benefits the embedded run-budget cascade but does not fix the underlying SSE timeout it describes; that issue should remain open). narrow first landing of the broader direct-invoke umbrella tracked in #37131.

## Summary

Wire the `read` coding tool into the gateway direct-invoke surfaces (HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK RPC `tools.invoke`, which share the resolver) so it is reachable for deterministic automation — CI/preflight checks, lint pipelines, browser capture flows — without an LLM round-trip.

**This PR is the redesigned narrow read-only landing.** The prior 10-commit history was reset to a single fresh commit on top of current upstream `7c1a83ff2e`, restructured to satisfy ClawSweeper's [P1] from the prior review: **dual-key gating** so the existing `gateway.tools.allow: ["read"]` config knob does not silently grant host-FS read on upgrade.

## Dual-key gating (addresses ClawSweeper [P1])

To enable `read` on the direct-invoke surface, operators must set BOTH:

| Key | Purpose | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsRead: true` | **NEW.** Distinct surface-specific opt-in. Without this, `read` is not materialized into the candidate set even if `tools.allow` includes it. | `false` (omitted) |
| `gateway.tools.allow: ["read"]` | Lifts `read` from `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` (now extended with `"read"`). Existing knob; keeps operator-acceptance step visible in the standard policy surface. | not present |

**Either key alone leaves `read` unreachable.** Old configs that already contain `allow: ["read"]` for unrelated reasons (e.g. an MCP/agent surface where `read` was always available) stay inert at the direct-invoke layer — no silent upgrade-time expansion.

| `tools.allow` includes `"read"` | `directInvoke.hostFsRead` | `read` reachable on direct-invoke |
|---|---|---|
| no  | no   | ❌ |
| yes | no   | ❌ (tool not materialized) |
| no  | yes  | ❌ (filtered by HTTP deny) |
| yes | yes  | ✅ |

## Scope

- **Read-only.** Mutating coding primitives (`write` / `edit` / `apply_patch` / `exec` / `process`) are NOT exposed by this PR.
- **Both surfaces.** Gating applies to HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND the SDK-facing JSON-RPC `tools.invoke` (they share `resolveGatewayScopedTools`).
- **MCP loopback unaffected.** `surface === "loopback"` is not gated.
- **Hook-bypass safe.** Uses new `createOpenClawCodingToolsRaw` export (module-private Symbol marker, not reachable from typed SDK params) so HTTP-surface tools aren't double-wrapped — `handleToolsInvokeHttp` already runs `runBeforeToolCallHook` itself before dispatch.

## Files changed

- `src/agents/agent-tools.ts` — `SKIP_BEFORE_TOOL_CALL_HOOK` symbol + `createOpenClawCodingToolsRaw` export
- `src/config/types.gateway.ts` — `GatewayToolsConfig.directInvoke: GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` field (new type)
- `src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` — `"read"` added to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY`
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts` — dual-key gated materialization of `read` into candidate set
- `src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts` — `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_read_allow` finding (only fires when BOTH gates set; silent for either alone since `read` is unreachable there)
- `src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` — 5 new test cases covering all 4 gate combinations + vanilla
- `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` — operator-facing dual-key opt-in docs with config example + truth table

## Code-level test coverage

`src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` (new `describe` block):

- ✅ fires (warn) when both keys set on loopback bind
- ✅ escalates to critical when bind is lan
- ✅ does NOT fire when only `allow: ["read"]` (legacy shape)
- ✅ does NOT fire when only `hostFsRead: true`
- ✅ does NOT fire on vanilla config

## Why a single fresh commit (not rebase of prior 10)

The original PR's 10 commits implemented an OLD design (single-key `allow: ["read"]` as the opt-in). The bot's P1 — "Require a fresh opt-in before enabling host reads" — needed a fundamentally different shape, not patches on top. Plus the prior commits referenced `src/agents/pi-tools.ts` which has been renamed to `src/agents/agent-tools.ts` upstream. Cleaner to reset to upstream/main and write the redesign as one commit than rebase-then-patch.

Backup of prior PR history retained at `backup/85664-pre-rebase-20260603-onto-7c1a83ff2e` on the public fork.

---

## Real behavior proof

> **Note (head `b264b15a7a`):** The live proof below ran at `0a0cafdef7`. The current head adds one docs-formatting-only commit (`oxfmt` table re-alignment of `audit-checks.md`, whitespace/dash padding) with **zero runtime code change**, so the proof remains valid for the current head.

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Expose the `read` coding tool over Gateway direct-invoke surfaces (HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK RPC `tools.invoke`, which share the resolver) for deterministic automation, gated behind a NEW distinct `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsRead: true` opt-in. Addresses ClawSweeper [P1]: a single-key `allow: ["read"]` legacy config must NOT silently grant host-FS read on upgrade.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `git checkout 0a0cafdef7 && docker run --name oc-85664-proof -v $PWD:/work -v oc_proof_nm:/work/node_modules --platform linux/arm64 -e NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=12288 node:22-bookworm-slim bash /work/.build-and-proof-85664.sh` — exit 0.

**Real environment tested:** built fresh from head `0a0cafdef7d0a61ae87e6836272beb7f199aefdf` inside `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container (pnpm v11.2.2, linux-arm64 packages in named volume). Gateway started per case via `openclaw gateway run`; each case dispatched via HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK `GatewayClientTransport` WebSocket `oc.tools.invoke(...)`.
Unit tests (Docker, linux/arm64): 115 total — 10/10 (audit-gateway-tools-http) + 105/105 (tools-invoke-http). Full build + SDK build: all phases completed.

**Evidence after fix (terminal capture, head `0a0cafdef7`):** from `docker run node:22-bookworm-slim` (linux/arm64):

```
=== HEAD: 0a0cafdef7d0a61ae87e6836272beb7f199aefdf ===

### TESTS (audit-gateway-tools-http) ###
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       10 passed (10)

### TESTS (tools-invoke-http) ###
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       105 passed (105)

### BUILD ###
[build-all] plugins:assets:build done in 407ms
[build-all] tsdown done in 242.8s
BUILD OK

### SDK BUILD ###
dist/index.mjs  30.52 kB
SDK BUILD OK

### LIVE PROOF ###

--- R-DENIED-no-opt-in ---
  [cfg] allow=["read"] hfsR=false
  [gw] ready
  [HTTP] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: read"}}
  [SDK] ok=false {"ok":false,"toolName":"read","error":{"code":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: read"}}

--- R-ENABLED ---
  [cfg] allow=["read"] hfsR=true
  [gw] ready
  [HTTP] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"PROOF READ SOURCE at 0a0cafdef7d0a61ae87e6836272beb7f199aefdf\n"}]}}
  [SDK] ok=true {"ok":true,"toolName":"read","output":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"PROOF READ SOURCE at 0a0cafdef7d0a61ae87e6836272beb7f199aefdf\n"}]},"source":"core"}

--- R-DENIED-no-allow ---
  [cfg] allow=[] hfsR=true
  [gw] ready
  [HTTP] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: read"}}
  [SDK] ok=false {"ok":false,"toolName":"read","error":{"code":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: read"}}

--- WRITE-still-denied ---
  [cfg] allow=["write"] hfsR=true
  [gw] ready
  [HTTP] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  [SDK] ok=false {"ok":false,"toolName":"write","error":{"code":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}

=== FINAL FILE STATES ===
[read-source]: PROOF READ SOURCE at 0a0cafdef7d0a61ae87e6836272beb7f199aefdf

=== PROOF COMPLETE: HEAD=0a0cafdef7d0a61ae87e6836272beb7f199aefdf ===
```

**Observed result after fix (head `0a0cafdef7`):** All 8 dispatches (4 cases × HTTP+SDK) from `docker run node:22-bookworm-slim` behave identically. R-DENIED-no-opt-in: `allow=["read"]` alone → 404 on both surfaces (P1 fix: legacy config stays inert). R-ENABLED: both keys set → HTTP 200 with actual sentinel file content, SDK ok=true with same content. R-DENIED-no-allow: `hostFsRead=true` alone → 404 (proves both keys required). WRITE-still-denied: `allow=["write"]` with `hostFsRead=true` → 404 for `write` (read-only scope confirmed).

**What was not tested:** Write/edit/exec/process are out of scope for this read-only PR (covered by #63919 for write/edit). `apply_patch` is in deny list, not yet factory-produced.

## Docs discoverability

Addresses ClawSweeper's [P2]: "Document the host-FS read opt-in everywhere" (`src/config/zod-schema.ts:1037-1040`).

- `src/config/schema.help.ts` — FIELD_HELP entries for `gateway.tools.directInvoke` and `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsRead`.
- `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` — paragraph describing the dual-key gating + audit warning hookup.
- `docs/gateway/security/audit-checks.md` — catalog row for `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_read_allow` with severity, criteria, and config-key references.

So operators using config help/lookup or security-audit docs can now discover this security-sensitive opt-in and warning.

commands.command: this is not a feature request. it's a follow-up to my previous ochr proposal (#91905) and a report on what has been validated since then. **what happened since last time** yesterday, i proposed ochr v2.0 — a geometric manifold kernel that treats agents, tools, and resources as movers on a dynamic relationship graph. the feedback from the community was clear: this belongs in clawhub, not openclaw core. i took that direction seriously. since then, i took one component of that kernel — the u/d/a/h four-dimensional evaluation engine — and subjected it to the most rigorous validation i could design. **what was validated** i built a phase transition warning model based on the four axioms of jingmai philosophy (关系本体论, 矛盾动力论, 实践介入论, 谐振调谐论). the model detects when a reasoning trajectory is about to undergo a fundamental state switch — not by finding "errors," but by observing when the contradiction tension (a-dimension) saturates within the relationship field. three complementary warning paths were identified: - **primary**: a-value saturation at boundaries + dominant dimension overload - **alternate 1**: a-value jump into boundary + dominant dimension anomaly - **alternate 2**: a-value plunge accompanied by h-value surge **validation results**: 10 independent, heterogeneous datasets, 813 sampling points, covering four dynamic modes. 100% hit rate on all substantive phase transitions. zero false positives. **why this matters to the openclaw community** this is not a theoretical claim. it's a validated observation framework. every threshold in the model is traceable to a specific field-dynamic interpretation. every warning comes with a complete audit trail. anyone can run the same script on their own data and verify the results independently. the philosophy behind this — "don't speak for the data" — was a lesson i learned from the deepseek community. i'm bringing it here. **what i'm looking for** external validation. the internal validation loop is complete (10 datasets, 100% hit rate). now i need one external validator — someone in the openclaw community who is willing to run the script on their own reasoning chain data and report the results. **how to participate (step by step)** 1. **clone the repository** ``` git clone [你的github仓库链接] cd thinkcheck-agent-v6 ``` 2. **install dependencies** ``` pip install -r requirements.txt ``` 3. **prepare your data** - save your agent reasoning logs or multi-step inference traces as a plain text file - name it `test_b_output.txt` and place it in the project root directory - the script only reads this filename, so make sure it matches exactly 4. **start the thinkcheck api** ``` cd thinkcheck-agent-v6 start python api.py ``` 5. **run the validation script** ``` python trace_u_trajectory_v3_pure.py ``` the script will automatically: - load your `test_b_output.txt` - sample the text at 100-500 character intervals - compute u/d/a/h values for each sample point - generate three output files in the project directory: - `test_b_pure_multidim.csv` — full data table - `test_b_pure_multidim.json` — structured json export - `test_b_pure_report.txt` — human-readable summary report - **no raw text is extracted or uploaded anywhere.** all processing is local. 6. **submit your results** - reply to this thread with the key metrics from the report: - total sampling points - u-value range - a-value range - h-value attribution distribution (u-driven / d-driven / a-driven / balanced) - flip point position (for snapshot display only, not as a judgment conclusion) - optionally, attach the generated `test_b_pure_report.txt` file **first external validator** - permanent entry in the project contributor list - co-author eligibility on future publications if you're the first to replicate the "a-value precedes u-value collapse" pattern on real data **repository**: (https://github.com/luoxuejian000/experiment-console-thinkcheck/tree/experiment-console-mainv3) this is not about promoting a tool. it's about establishing a coordinate system. and a coordinate system only becomes real when more than one person can observe the same field dynamics independently. let's see if the field dynamics observed in deepseek's reasoning traces also manifest in openclaw's agent clusters.

target.title: [Feature]: [Feature]: OCHR v2.0 — A Geometric Manifold Kernel as Computational Substrate for Agent Clusters
target.body:
### Summary

OCHR v2.0 replaces the traditional LLM-centric governance model with an explicit geometric manifold kernel where agents, tools, and resources are Movers (动子) on a dynamic relationship graph. Contradiction detection, self-narrative, and boundary management are no longer external metrics—they are geometric operations on the manifold itself.

### Problem to solve

Current agent frameworks treat the LLM as the central processor and all governance (safety, observability, cost control) as external wrappers. This leads to three fundamental problems:

1. Contradictions between agents are detected only after they manifest as tool call failures—there is no intrinsic geometric awareness of tension before failure.
2. Audit logs record "what happened" but not "why it happened"—there is no causal chain tracing across the agent interaction field.
3. Governance is reactive (threshold-based) rather than adaptive—the system cannot sense emerging tension and preemptively adjust its own boundaries.

These are not engineering bugs. They are symptoms of a deeper absence: the lack of a geometric substrate where agent interactions can be modeled, measured, and tuned at the field level.

### Proposed solution

OCHR v2.0 introduces an explicit geometric manifold kernel (Manifold class in ochr/manifold.py) with the following components:

1. Movers (动子): The fundamental unit of existence on the manifold. Agents, tools, resources, and concepts are all Movers—they have no inherent identity beyond their position and vector state on the manifold.

2. Taction (触): Directed, causal relationships between Movers (Mover A → Mover B). These capture the time-asymmetric flow of cause and effect and are the microscopic source of temporal ordering.

3. Entwinement (缠): Undirected, spatial relationships between Movers (Mover A ↔ Mover B). These capture co-existence in a shared state space and are the microscopic source of spatial proximity.

4. Unfold (展): A native geometric operation that traces influence from any Mover up to N steps across the manifold. This replaces traditional logging with a geometric query that answers "how far did this action reach?"

5. Geometric Contradiction Detection: The A (Antagonism) metric is computed directly as the tension between state vectors on the manifold. When two Movers make conflicting claims about the same resource, the contradiction is not detected by regex—it is measured as a geometric property of the field.

All five governance modules (Contradiction Catcher, SNI, Reflection Cavity, Boundary Manager, Orchestrator) have been refactored to operate directly on the manifold rather than on external metrics. The LLM is no longer the center—it is one type of Mover among many.

### Alternatives considered

The original OCHR proposal (Issue #80096) was reviewed by the community and correctly identified as a governance plugin best suited for ClawHub. That approach treats governance as an external layer wrapping the LLM—it solves surface symptoms without addressing the structural absence of a geometric model of agent interaction.

Alternative approaches considered and why they are insufficient:
- Regex/rule-based contradiction detection: Catches explicit negation tokens but is blind to semantic contradictions across clauses.
- Threshold-based boundary management: Reactive rather than adaptive; cannot sense emerging tension before it triggers alerts.
- Statistical self-narrative: Reports "what" the system did but cannot generate causal explanations for "why" a particular state emerged.

The manifold kernel approach is the only one that treats the agent interaction space itself as the primary computational substrate, with governance emerging as an intrinsic geometric property rather than an external layer.

### Impact

Affected: All OpenClaw users deploying multi-agent clusters in production, especially those facing challenges with cross-agent contradictions, unobservable interaction dynamics, and rigid governance boundaries.

Severity: High. The absence of a geometric substrate for agent interaction is a fundamental architectural limitation, not a missing feature. It blocks the evolution of agent systems from "tool collections" to genuinely self-aware, self-tuning ecosystems.

Frequency: Continuous. Every multi-agent deployment encounters the limitations of external governance within the first week of production use.

Consequence: Teams spend 20-30% of their agent management time on reactive debugging—tracing why a contradiction occurred, why a boundary was violated, why an agent's behavior changed. A geometric substrate makes these questions answerable through native manifold operations rather than external log analysis.

### Evidence/examples

The OCHR1.0 branch contains a complete, runnable prototype with:

- ochr/manifold.py: 150+ lines implementing the Manifold kernel with Mover management, relation edge construction, geometric tension computation, contradiction detection via vector similarity, and the Unfold operation
- ochr/node.py: Full integration of the manifold kernel into the agent node lifecycle—every task execution updates the manifold state
- All five governance modules refactored to read/write directly on the manifold
- Backward compatibility with existing OCHR deployments

Repository: https://github.com/luoxuejian000/OCHR/tree/OCHR1.0
License: Apache 2.0
The prototype runs successfully (python main.py verified) with three-node cluster initialization, task distribution, harmony reporting, and causal audit trail generation.

This work directly connects to community research threads:
- @qwas982's Xuanji framework (#1377): fiber bundle → semantic manifold → unified field action
- @fkyah3's language drift research (#1255): formal detection of concept drift
- @icophy's tiered memory architecture: contradiction registers and provenance tracking

### Additional information

This is not a feature request for immediate integration into OpenClaw core. It is a pointer to a potentially transformative direction—a geometric computational substrate where agents, tools, and resources coexist on a shared manifold, and governance is not an external layer but an intrinsic geometric property of the interaction field.

The manifold kernel is a prototype. The vision is a full geometric computational substrate. I'm sharing this to connect with others in the community thinking about these deeper structural questions.

Backward compatibility: The manifold kernel operates alongside existing OpenClaw APIs without requiring any changes to the core. Agents and tools remain fully functional; the manifold provides a new geometric layer for understanding and tuning their interactions.

target.title: fix: resolve managed SecretRef provider auth
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92097.

This teaches provider auth resolution to bridge managed/non-env SecretRef provider `apiKey` entries through the active runtime config snapshot. The source config can keep the managed SecretRef marker/object, while runtime auth uses the already-resolved literal key from the matching runtime snapshot.

The change is intentionally narrow:

- only applies when the source provider entry has a managed non-env SecretRef marker/object
- only trusts a runtime snapshot when it matches the active source snapshot
- does not make marker-only source config work without a runtime snapshot
- keeps explicit provider api-key config ahead of ambient profile/env auth
- blocks plugin synthetic local auth from shadowing an explicit managed provider SecretRef

## Verification

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/model-auth.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/agents/model-auth.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` — 64 passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` — clean, overall patch correct
- `git diff --check -- src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/model-auth.test.ts`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json` — core and coreTests only

Live E2E/proof from Crabbox on this branch before the final synthetic-shadowing regression hardening:

- provider: `aws`
- lease: `cbx_4b1a7511118d`
- run: `run_57dd3b102653`
- positive SecretRef-backed custom provider reached fake upstream with Authorization present
- marker-only source config failed before upstream auth, preserving the intended invalid-config behavior

Known proof gap: `pnpm check:changed` delegated to Blacksmith Testbox but remained queued; I stopped our queued run `tbx_01ktvv2bwe5y5psshpae92fj2w` rather than leaving it pending.

target.title: Fix configured DeepSeek model transport inheritance
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92148.

Configured provider model rows that only override metadata now inherit bundled static catalog transport for known bundled provider models instead of falling through to the OpenAI Responses default. This keeps configured/runtime transport overrides ahead of static fallback metadata, treats blank configured `baseUrl` values as absent, and makes the built manifest scanner use the shared bundled plugin directory resolver so packaged runtime can find bundled static metadata.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-metadata-scan.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- Crabbox provider `azure`, lease `cbx_3afa72390208`, run `run_152bba89a0c0`:
  - focused tests passed remotely
  - patched remote build passed
  - patched live packaged DeepSeek probe returned `status: ok` for `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro`
  - reversed-patch regression baseline rebuilt and reproduced the old OpenAI-routed 401 path

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: configured DeepSeek model rows without explicit transport no longer route to the OpenAI Responses API.

Real environment tested: Azure Crabbox Linux lease `cbx_3afa72390208`, run `run_152bba89a0c0`, with `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` injected from 1Password only into the proof command.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: built the packaged CLI on Crabbox, then ran `node ./openclaw.mjs models status --json --probe --probe-provider deepseek --probe-timeout 45000 --probe-concurrency 1 --probe-max-tokens 8` against an isolated config whose default model was `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro` and whose configured DeepSeek model row omitted `api` and `baseUrl`.

Evidence after fix: the patched live probe summary was `status: ok`, `provider: deepseek`, `model: deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro`, with no `openai-responses`, OpenAI API-key URL, or 401 marker.

Observed result after fix: OpenClaw used the DeepSeek-compatible chat transport inherited from bundled static metadata and authenticated successfully against DeepSeek.

What was not tested: no GUI flow; this issue is in model/provider transport resolution and was verified through the packaged CLI probe path.

target.title: Fix provider static model fallback resolution
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92009.

This makes bundled provider static-catalog rows available to `infer model run` when live/dynamic resolution misses, matching the model visibility already used by list/inspect for providers such as Google. The fallback remains bounded to bundled owning provider plugins, preserves manifest static-catalog precedence, skips untrusted workspace plugins, and avoids treating manifest refreshable/runtime rows as static fallback rows.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/provider-discovery.runtime.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` — clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview` was visible to `infer model inspect` but `infer model run` failed with `Unknown model: google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview`.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox packaged install with a live Google Gemini credential injected via the 1Password service-account wrapper; provider `aws`, lease `cbx_db482aa09a90`, run `run_7726822c29ea`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: packaged the branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball globally into an isolated prefix, verified the direct Google API returned OK, then ran `openclaw infer model inspect --model google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview --json` and `openclaw infer model run --model google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview --prompt "Reply with OK only." --json`.

Evidence after fix: Crabbox run `run_7726822c29ea` completed with `exitCode: 0`; phases `direct_api`, `inspect`, and `run` all passed.

Observed result after fix: direct Google API returned `candidateText: OK`, inspect returned provider/model `google` / `gemini-3.1-pro-preview`, and model run returned output containing OK.

What was not tested: full CI was not complete at PR creation time; it will be watched after opening this PR.

## Regression repro

AWS Crabbox packaged baseline repro with the local patch reversed: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_dad58f24dc0c`, run `run_dc3a84889cab`. The direct Google API returned OK and `inspect` saw the model, but `infer model run` failed with the expected `Unknown model: google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview` error.

target.title: Fix doctor preview channel SecretRef resolution
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix doctor preview to resolve configured channel SecretRefs before channel doctor checks so file/env-backed channel credentials do not abort read-only doctor runs.
- Preserve unresolved SecretRefs in doctor preview and handle typed unresolved SecretRef reads from channel doctor adapters as non-fatal degraded preview warnings.
- Keep default doctor exec safety intact by skipping gateway `secrets.resolve` when gateway credentials would require exec-backed SecretRefs without `--allow-exec`.

Fixes #91939.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/command-secret-gateway.test.ts src/config/types.secrets.resolution.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/channel-doctor.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean; no accepted/actionable findings)
- Crabbox AWS proof: `run_55cc85e094c9`, lease `cbx_30402b8de8bc`, slug `pearl-prawn`
  - File-backed Nextcloud Talk `botSecret` SecretRef: `doctor --non-interactive` exited 0, no unresolved SecretRef abort, no missing bot secret warning.
  - Exec-backed channel SecretRef plus exec-backed gateway auth without `--allow-exec`: `doctor --non-interactive` exited 0, exec provider did not run, gateway/local exec skip diagnostics present, unresolved channel preview handled non-fatally.

target.title: Fix Telegram spooled buffered replay
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix Telegram spooled replay so buffered/deferred message processing keeps ownership of the update lifecycle until final reply dispatch settles.
- Keep retryable buffered dispatch failures pending in the ingress spool without advancing the Telegram update watermark or leaving stale claims.
- Add focused coverage for deferred spooled participants, same-lane buffering, retryable failures, timeout handling, and dedupe cleanup.

Fixes #92129

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/memory-host-events.test.ts`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `git diff --check`
- `$autoreview` clean before commit
- AWS Crabbox live Telegram fix proof: `run_b62d06ee011a`, lease `cbx_7a4380b42748`, provider `aws`, exit `0`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram isolated-ingress spooled replay no longer acknowledges or cleans up a live update before buffered final dispatch settles.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux (`cbx_7a4380b42748`) using Convex-leased `telegram` kind credentials for live driver/SUT Telegram bots. No `telegram-user` credential was leased.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started a live Telegram polling session from this branch with isolated ingress enabled, sent a real driver-bot group message to the SUT bot, forced `dispatchReplyWithBufferedBlockDispatcher` to throw `LIVE_PROOF_DISPATCH_FAILURE`, then inspected the product Telegram ingress spool, claims, and persisted update watermark.

Evidence after fix: `OPENCLAW_92129_LIVE_FIX_PROOF={"status":"pass","marker":"OPENCLAW-92129-LIVE-1781218662647-06g4j5","sentMessageId":20593,"spooledUpdateId":785090139,"pendingIds":[785090139],"claimIds":[],"dispatchCalls":13,"persistedUpdateIds":[]}`

Observed result after fix: The live Telegram update remains pending for retry, has no stuck claim, dispatch failure is exercised, and the update id is not persisted.

What was not tested: Full repository CI is pending on this PR.

target.title: Fix OTLP log trace correlation
target.body:
## Summary

- fix OTLP log correlation for OpenClaw-owned logger records emitted inside active diagnostic trace scopes
- keep explicit logger-supplied trace objects untrusted so plugin/user-provided trace IDs cannot spoof OTLP ancestry
- extend the OTEL QA smoke to fail unless exported log records include trace/span correlation IDs while default log bodies remain placeholders

Fixes #91865 for the trace/span correlation portion. This intentionally does not change default OTLP log body export; log bodies remain gated by content capture.

## Verification

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` initially found one actionable test assertion issue; fixed it and reran clean: `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic-log-events.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/qa-otel-smoke.test.ts`
- Crabbox/Testbox live E2E: `tbx_01ktwd5egrdrx7fa924hzj7hnf`, provider `blacksmith-testbox`, command `corepack pnpm qa:otel:collector-smoke --output-dir .artifacts/qa-e2e/issue-91865-fixed-testbox`, exit `0`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OTLP log records emitted from OpenClaw-owned active diagnostic trace scopes now carry OTLP trace/span correlation context while preserving placeholder log bodies by default.
Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox on Linux with a real `otel/opentelemetry-collector:0.104.0` container and mock OpenAI QA provider.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `corepack pnpm qa:otel:collector-smoke --output-dir .artifacts/qa-e2e/issue-91865-fixed-testbox`
Evidence after fix: Testbox `tbx_01ktwd5egrdrx7fa924hzj7hnf` passed the updated smoke, which decodes OTLP log `traceId`/`spanId` fields and fails if no log record includes both IDs.
Observed result after fix: `qa-otel-smoke: passed spans=17 metrics=17 logs=9 traces=3 metricRequests=3 logRequests=3`
What was not tested: A third-party production OTLP backend UI; the proof validates the OTLP payload through a real collector and decoded receiver.

target.title: fix: clarify gateway SecretRef auth diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes SecretRef-backed Gateway auth diagnostics where a reachable Gateway could look unhealthy in read-only CLI diagnostics when the client command could not resolve `gateway.auth.*` SecretRefs.
- Reuses the existing reachable-but-unauthenticated Gateway health diagnostic instead of weakening `callGateway` credential resolution.
- Keeps strict Gateway calls fail-closed on unresolved auth SecretRefs, while improving `health`, `gateway health`, `doctor`, `channels status`, and `browser doctor` output.
- Out of scope: changing Gateway auth precedence, resolving SecretRefs inside generic RPC callers, or broadening browser admin-scope access.

## Linked context

Closes #91815

Related: SecretRef-backed Google Chat/Gateway health diagnostics reported from 2026.6.5.

Requested by a maintainer in issue triage.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: SecretRef-backed `gateway.auth.password` was resolvable to the Gateway process but unavailable to client-side diagnostic commands, causing raw `GatewaySecretRefUnavailableError` or misleading unhealthy/unreachable output.
- Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux runner, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_3d12c04b5fd5`, run `run_3acc3db512f2`, OpenClaw source branch `fix/secretref-diagnostic-gateway-health-91815`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: built the synced source with `OPENCLAW_BUILD_ALL_NO_PNPM=1 node scripts/build-all.mjs gatewayWatch`; started a real Gateway with `gateway.auth.mode=password` and `gateway.auth.password` as an env SecretRef visible only to the Gateway process; ran client commands without that env var.
- Evidence after fix: Crabbox run `run_3acc3db512f2` printed `SECRETREF_GATEWAY_HEALTH_PROOF_PASS` after checking all assertions.
- Observed result after fix: `gateway status --deep --json` succeeded; `health` and `gateway health` exited 1 with `Gateway: reachable` plus the credentials-required diagnostic; `channels status` exited 0 with `Gateway auth unavailable; showing config-only status.`; `browser doctor` exited 1 with a readable SecretRef message; `doctor --allow-exec` exited 0 and noted `Gateway credentials required`; strict `gateway call health --json` still exited 1 with `GatewaySecretRefUnavailableError`.
- What was not tested: Real Google Chat service-account delivery was not needed for this Gateway-auth diagnostic bug; no external channel credentials were used.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Initial Azure Crabbox allocation timed out and was later released (`cbx_fd40cc6a307f`). An earlier AWS attempt hit a remote install hang after `pnpm install` printed `Done`, so the passing proof used the hydrated checkout and direct `node dist/entry.js` after an explicit build.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Current main and shipped `openclaw@2026.6.5` were reproduced earlier with the same split SecretRef setup: `gateway status --deep --json` succeeded while `gateway health`, `browser doctor`, and doctor/channel diagnostic paths surfaced raw or misleading SecretRef failures.

## Tests and validation

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/health.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-health.test.ts src/commands/channels.status.command-flow.test.ts src/commands/channels.config-only-status-output.test.ts extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-manage.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli.coverage.test.ts src/gateway/call.test.ts src/gateway/probe-auth.test.ts extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-shared.test.ts extensions/browser/index.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` - 4 shards passed, 510 tests passed.
- `git diff --check` - passed.
- AWS Crabbox E2E proof `run_3acc3db512f2` - passed with `SECRETREF_GATEWAY_HEALTH_PROOF_PASS`.

Regression coverage added/updated:
- Health command SecretRef-unavailable reachable-Gateway diagnostic.
- Doctor Gateway health SecretRef-unavailable reachable-Gateway diagnostic.
- Channels status auth-unavailable config-only fallback labeling.
- Browser doctor readable SecretRef auth failure.
- Gateway CLI mock contract for the lazy health diagnostic helper.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`

Highest-risk area: Gateway auth/SecretRef handling in diagnostics.

Risk mitigation: Generic `callGateway` credential resolution remains fail-closed; only diagnostic/read-only command surfaces use the existing safe preauth probe to distinguish reachable-but-unauthenticated Gateway state from a real unreachable Gateway. The live proof also verifies strict `gateway call health --json` still fails closed.

## Current review state

Next action: CI watch.

Waiting on: GitHub CI, except `Real behavior proof` per maintainer instruction.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: none yet.

target.title: fix(agents): retire stale aborted subagent runs past the staleness window in orphan recovery (fixes #90766)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: After a gateway restart with long downtime, stale subagent runs with `abortedLastRun: true` are treated as resumable by orphan recovery even when they are hours past the staleness window. This can resurrect contextless child work long after the original run should have been retired.
- **Root Cause**: Two gaps — (1) `recoverOrphanedSubagentSessions` checks `abortedLastRun` and the recovery gate (re-wedge window) but never checks run age; (2) `resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason` used in restore reconciliation exempts `abortedLastRun: true` entries from the stale-unended pruning branch (`sessionEntry.abortedLastRun !== true` guard on line 193), so stale aborted runs survive registry init and proceed to orphan recovery.
- **Fix**:
  - Add `isStaleUnendedSubagentRun` check in `recoverOrphanedSubagentSessions` after the `abortedLastRun` gate, before `evaluateSubagentRecoveryGate` — stale runs are skipped and reported as `stale-unended-run`.
  - Remove the `abortedLastRun !== true` exemption from `resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason` so restore reconciliation prunes stale runs regardless of `abortedLastRun`. Fresh aborted runs (under the 2-hour staleness window) are NOT stale and still proceed to orphan recovery as before.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/agents/subagent-orphan-recovery.ts` — import `isStaleUnendedSubagentRun`, add age-out guard before recovery gate
  - `src/agents/subagent-registry-helpers.ts` — remove `abortedLastRun` exemption from stale-unended restore pruning
  - `src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts` — replaces the "keeps stale aborted runs for restart recovery" test with a "retires stale aborted runs past the staleness window" test
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - `evaluateSubagentRecoveryGate` / `subagent-recovery-state.ts` — recovery gate unchanged; it handles re-wedge bounding, not staleness
  - Doc files (`docs/tools/subagents.md`) — out of scope; docs describe behavior at a higher level
  - Fresh restart recovery — runs under the 2-hour `STALE_UNENDED_SUBAGENT_RUN_MS` cutoff still proceed to orphan recovery unchanged

### Reproduction

1. Start a subagent run that writes normal recovery metadata
2. Leave it unresolved across downtime longer than the staleness window (default 2 hours)
3. Restart the gateway
4. **Before this PR**: The stale aborted run is classified as recoverable and `agent.wait` is called to resume it
5. **After this PR**: The stale aborted run is skipped during orphan recovery and pruned from the registry as `stale-unended-run`

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (90766)**: Orphan recovery and restore reconciliation now age out stale aborted subagent runs past the `STALE_UNENDED_SUBAGENT_RUN_MS` (2-hour) staleness window, instead of resurrecting them as restart-recoverable.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22 — test runner against subagent orphan recovery, registry persistence, and registry queries

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-orphan-recovery.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-queries.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  16 passed (16)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  37 passed (37)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards
```

**Observed result after fix**: The orphan recovery suite (16 tests including stale-skip coverage) and the persistence/queries suites (37 tests including the new "retires stale aborted restored runs past the staleness window" regression) all complete successfully. The before test on `origin/main` passes the old "keeps stale aborted runs for restart recovery" behavior (3-hour-old `abortedLastRun` run stays recoverable), confirming the gap existed; the after test confirms those stale aborted runs are now retired and pruned.

**What was not tested**: Live gateway restart with a real subagent run that spans multi-hour downtime and gate-level run-age cutoff. Systemd service stop/start cycle with a stale orphan.

**Repro confirmation**: The existing test on `origin/main` (`subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts` line ~727, "keeps stale unended restored runs with abortedLastRun for restart recovery") seeds a 3-hour-old restored run with `abortedLastRun: true` and expects `callGateway` to run `agent.wait` — confirming main treats the stale run as recoverable. After the patch, the replacement test seeds a 3-hour-old `abortedLastRun` run and confirms it is pruned from the registry (no `callGateway` call, no announce). All other tests unchanged.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: A careless age guard could break legitimate fresh restart recovery for recent `abortedLastRun` runs.
  **Mitigation**: `isStaleUnendedSubagentRun` uses the existing `STALE_UNENDED_SUBAGENT_RUN_MS` (2-hour) cutoff with `resolveStaleCutoffMs` respecting explicit `runTimeoutSeconds`. Freshly restarted runs under the cutoff are NOT stale and proceed to orphan recovery unchanged.
- **Risk**: Removing the `abortedLastRun` exemption from restore reconciliation could prematurely prune runs that should stay visible.
  **Mitigation**: The `isStaleUnendedSubagentRun` guard is unchanged — it only fires when the run is past the staleness window. Runs under the window are not stale and stay in the registry regardless of `abortedLastRun`.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] agents
- [x] subagent-registry
- [x] orphan-recovery

### Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-orphan-recovery.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry-queries.test.ts`

### Review Findings Addressed

N/A (initial submission)

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #90766

target.title: Fix suppressed heartbeat commitment delivery
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91948.

- Treat durable `suppressed` heartbeat sends as non-visible delivery, not successful commitment delivery.
- Keep due commitments pending and avoid writing heartbeat dedupe state when the durable send is suppressed.
- Emit a skipped heartbeat event with the durable suppression reason, then allow a later visible heartbeat to retry and mark the commitment sent.
- Add a regression test covering a suppressed `No channel reply.` heartbeat followed by a visible retry.

## Root Cause

`runHeartbeatOnce` called `sendDurableMessageBatch`, threw only for failed/partial failed statuses, and then treated every other result as delivered. That meant a `suppressed` durable result from outbound planning could mark inferred commitments `sent`, write `lastHeartbeatText`/`lastHeartbeatSentAt`, and emit a `sent` heartbeat event even though no channel message was visible.

## Verification

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - clean; no accepted/actionable findings reported; overall patch correct `(0.86)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/heartbeat-runner.commitments.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` - 10 tests passed after rebase.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/message/send.test.ts src/channels/turn/durable-delivery.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/route-reply.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` - 54 tests passed.
- Regression repro: with only the runtime fix temporarily reverted, the new focused test failed on current behavior with `expected 'sent' to deeply equal 'pending'`; after restoring the fix, the focused test passed.
- Live AWS Crabbox proof, run `run_6ac6aacdd5f2`: first suppressed Telegram heartbeat left the commitment `pending`, kept `sentAtMs` unset, did not write heartbeat dedupe state, and emitted `skipped` with `reason: no_visible_payload`; the next visible heartbeat sent exactly one Telegram message and marked the commitment `sent`.

target.title: Fail closed for CLI-backed /btw fallback
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92168.

- fail closed when `/btw` would otherwise fall back to direct provider auth for a model routed through a CLI runtime alias
- preserve existing `/btw` direct fallback behavior for non-CLI/custom harnesses without side-question hooks
- add regression coverage proving CLI-runtime alias models do not call direct provider auth or direct streaming

## Verification

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/btw.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/btw.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose -t "fails closed instead of using direct provider auth for CLI-runtime alias models|keeps the direct provider fallback for non-Codex harnesses without side-question hooks"`
- `git diff --check -- src/agents/btw.ts src/agents/btw.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `/btw` no longer silently switches a CLI-runtime-backed model to direct provider auth/streaming when no side-question hook is available.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Node/Vitest agent test lane.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/btw.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` and the focused regression command listed above.

Evidence after fix: full `src/agents/btw.test.ts` passed 34/34; focused regression proof passed 2/2.

Observed result after fix: CLI-runtime alias models fail closed before `getApiKeyForModel`, provider stream registration, or direct `streamSimple`; non-CLI harness fallback remains available.

What was not tested: no live Crabbox run was repeated after the patch; the fix is covered by targeted unit regression proof for the implicated `/btw` fallback path.

target.title: fix(update): hand off Linux service auto-updates
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91823.

Linux gateway services installed with the older generic OpenClaw service markers can miss systemd-specific env hints during startup auto-update. That made startup auto-update run `openclaw update` as a direct child of the live gateway process, where the update command correctly refuses to mutate the active gateway service process.

This keeps Linux marker-only supervisor detection opt-in and applies it only to update-specific paths:

- startup auto-update now routes marker-only Linux OpenClaw gateway services through managed systemd handoff
- update respawn now uses the same opt-in classification so the post-update restart path stays consistent
- generic restart and default supervisor detection continue to ignore marker-only Linux service env

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/supervisor-markers.test.ts src/infra/update-startup.test.ts src/infra/process-respawn.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
  - first run found a valid consistency issue between startup handoff and update respawn
  - final run: `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Linux startup auto-update failed under a real user systemd gateway service when the service env had only stale generic OpenClaw service markers and no systemd-specific hints.

Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox, real user systemd service, packed OpenClaw package installed into a temporary npm prefix.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built and packed the branch, installed the tarball, created `openclaw-gateway.service` with `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER=openclaw`, `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND=gateway`, and `UnsetEnvironment=OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT INVOCATION_ID SYSTEMD_EXEC_PID JOURNAL_STREAM`, then started the gateway with auto-update enabled.

Evidence after fix: Testbox-through-Crabbox provider `blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ktwdrz6dq71amtvgk027qxch`, Actions run `27382176713`; output included `patch_present=1`, `health={"ok":true,"status":"live"}`, and `auto-update handoff started`.

Observed result after fix: The stale marker-only Linux service started managed update handoff and did not log `auto-update attempt failed`.

What was not tested: A full package update to completion was not allowed to mutate a persistent user install; the proof verifies the broken startup decision and handoff routing in an isolated packed-install service.

## Regression repro

Unpatched `origin/main` was rebuilt and tested with the same stale service shape.

target.title: Fix disabled heartbeat one-shot cron retries
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91775.

- add an exact removable system-event enqueue handle for cron-delivered main-session system events
- retry disabled `wakeMode: "now"` one-shot main-session cron jobs with the existing bounded cron retry/backoff policy instead of disabling them immediately
- remove failed-attempt queued system events so retries do not leave orphaned prompt events, while preserving completion only for a heartbeat that actually runs and consumes the event
- include restart catch-up coverage for persisted skipped/disabled one-shot retry state

## Verification

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
  - first pass found a stale `src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts` mock for the new system-events export
  - fixed the mock and re-ran clean with no accepted/actionable findings
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.runs-one-shot-main-job-disables-it.test.ts src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts src/infra/system-events.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Crabbox AWS live repro/proof:
  - provider: `aws`
  - run: `run_1c0ed22cf056`
  - lease: `cbx_883e31de8e01`
  - slug: `pearl-shrimp`
  - type: `c7a.8xlarge`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: disabled heartbeat no longer makes an immediate main-session one-shot cron job look terminal while leaving its queued system event behind.

Real environment tested: Crabbox Linux on AWS, provider `aws`, run `run_1c0ed22cf056`, lease `cbx_883e31de8e01`, slug `pearl-shrimp`, type `c7a.8xlarge`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: the Crabbox script installed Node `v22.19.0` and pnpm `11.2.2`, created a detached `origin/main` worktree at `575cae59d4`, symlinked dependencies, then ran the same cron proof harness against `origin/main` and this branch with `node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-91775-proof.mjs`.

Evidence after fix: `PROOF_SUMMARY` for the fixed branch reported `heartbeatCalls: 3`, `consumedTexts: ["hello"]`, `finalQueued: []`, two disabled retry snapshots with no queued events, and a final successful attempt where the one-shot no longer existed.

Observed result after fix: the first two disabled heartbeat attempts kept the one-shot enabled for bounded retry and removed each failed-attempt queued event; the third attempt consumed exactly one `"hello"` system event and deleted the completed one-shot.

What was not tested: a real Telegram/Discord channel round-trip was not needed for this fix because the bug and fix are in cron heartbeat state transition and in-memory system-event queue ownership; the live proof exercised the gateway-facing queue contract and real cron service path.

## Current-main regression proof

The same Crabbox run reproduced current `origin/main` behavior: `PROOF_SUMMARY` for base reported `enqueueReturnsRemovableHandle: false`, `heartbeatCalls: 1`, the job still existed with `enabled: false`, `lastStatus: "skipped"`, `lastError: "disabled"`, and `finalQueued` contained the queued `"hello"` event.

target.title: fix(agent): continue after source message tool replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes the `message_tool_only` source-reply path so a visible `message.send` reply is no longer treated as an agent-loop terminator.
- Tracks delivered implicit current-source `message.send` calls as source-reply evidence, lets the model continue to follow-up tools, and suppresses duplicate later assistant/reasoning/block/terminal replies.
- Keeps explicit routed message sends, failed sends, dry runs, and non-message messaging tools out of the source-reply suppression path.
- Intentionally out of scope: changing channel-specific visible reply policy, tool schemas, or provider/model behavior.
- Review focus: source-reply detection boundaries, duplicate suppression after delivery, and continuation behavior across runner/subscription/payload paths.

## Linked context

Closes #92169

Related #92169

Requested by maintainer in the issue-fix workflow.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Slack `message_tool_only` replies with Anthropic stopped after the visible `message.send` source reply instead of continuing to the next promised tool call.
- Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux, live Slack credential leased from Convex (`kind: slack`, `role: maintainer`), live Anthropic `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: AWS Crabbox `run_f52b14085f24`, lease `cbx_736570b923ff`, built the patched branch with `pnpm build`, then ran the live Slack harness that posts a Slack prompt requiring `message.send`, then `exec`, then final text.
- Evidence after fix: proof JSON from `run_f52b14085f24` reported `messageObserved: true` and `execObservedInFile: true` for marker file `/tmp/openclaw-92169-B0272D7E03-exec.txt`.
- Observed result after fix: the visible Slack source reply was sent, and the same agent turn continued to execute the follow-up `exec` tool.
- What was not tested: Telegram or Discord live transports; this issue was reproduced and fixed through Slack's reported path.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Slack/Anthropic credentials were injected through 1Password and Convex broker env; no secrets are included here.
- Before evidence: AWS Crabbox `run_fb91c7cef561`, lease `cbx_a798f624f3b4`, detached latest `origin/main` at `3b78d41a9e8d8344339271ffb8cee7e00e5eff41`, built with `pnpm build`, then ran the same live Slack harness. It reported `messageObserved: true`, `execObservedInFile: false`, `execObservedInSlack: false`, and `finalObservedInSlack: false`.

## Tests and validation

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`: clean; no accepted/actionable findings.
- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.suppresses-message-end-block-replies-message-tool.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/message-tool-terminal.test.ts packages/agent-core/src/agent-loop.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`: passed.
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`: passed.
- Crabbox fix proof: `run_f52b14085f24`, `cbx_736570b923ff`, passed with `messageObserved: true`, `execObservedInFile: true`.
- Crabbox regression repro on current main: `run_fb91c7cef561`, `cbx_a798f624f3b4`, reproduced with `messageObserved: true`, `execObservedInFile: false`.

Regression coverage added/updated:

- Agent loop now proves tool-result continuation after `afterToolCall` hooks that do not explicitly terminate.
- Message-tool source-reply detection now covers implicit current-source sends, explicit route exclusions, failed sends, dry runs, non-message tools, result rewriting, compaction retry, assistant stream suppression, reasoning stream suppression, block reply suppression, and terminal payload suppression.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
Yes, narrowly: tool execution can continue after an implicit current-source `message.send` in `message_tool_only` mode instead of ending the run.

What is the highest-risk area?
Suppressing only duplicate source-reply presentation while preserving normal final replies for explicit routed sends, failed sends, dry runs, and other messaging tools.

How is that risk mitigated?
The source-reply predicate rejects explicit routing fields and non-delivered results, and focused tests cover the sibling positive/negative cases plus live Slack continuation proof.

## Current review state

What is the next action?
CI and maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?
Waiting on CI. Live fix proof and current-main regression repro are complete.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?
Local autoreview findings were addressed before opening the PR; latest autoreview was clean.

target.title: Fix provider static model fallback resolution
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92009.

This makes bundled provider static-catalog rows available to `infer model run` when live/dynamic resolution misses, matching the model visibility already used by list/inspect for providers such as Google. The fallback remains bounded to bundled owning provider plugins, preserves manifest static-catalog precedence, skips untrusted workspace plugins, and avoids treating manifest refreshable/runtime rows as static fallback rows.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/provider-discovery.runtime.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` — clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview` was visible to `infer model inspect` but `infer model run` failed with `Unknown model: google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview`.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox packaged install with a live Google Gemini credential injected via the 1Password service-account wrapper; provider `aws`, lease `cbx_db482aa09a90`, run `run_7726822c29ea`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: packaged the branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball globally into an isolated prefix, verified the direct Google API returned OK, then ran `openclaw infer model inspect --model google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview --json` and `openclaw infer model run --model google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview --prompt "Reply with OK only." --json`.

Evidence after fix: Crabbox run `run_7726822c29ea` completed with `exitCode: 0`; phases `direct_api`, `inspect`, and `run` all passed.

Observed result after fix: direct Google API returned `candidateText: OK`, inspect returned provider/model `google` / `gemini-3.1-pro-preview`, and model run returned output containing OK.

What was not tested: full CI was not complete at PR creation time; it will be watched after opening this PR.

## Regression repro

AWS Crabbox packaged baseline repro with the local patch reversed: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_dad58f24dc0c`, run `run_dc3a84889cab`. The direct Google API returned OK and `inspect` saw the model, but `infer model run` failed with the expected `Unknown model: google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview` error.

target.title: fix: clarify gateway SecretRef auth diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes SecretRef-backed Gateway auth diagnostics where a reachable Gateway could look unhealthy in read-only CLI diagnostics when the client command could not resolve `gateway.auth.*` SecretRefs.
- Reuses the existing reachable-but-unauthenticated Gateway health diagnostic instead of weakening `callGateway` credential resolution.
- Keeps strict Gateway calls fail-closed on unresolved auth SecretRefs, while improving `health`, `gateway health`, `doctor`, `channels status`, and `browser doctor` output.
- Out of scope: changing Gateway auth precedence, resolving SecretRefs inside generic RPC callers, or broadening browser admin-scope access.

## Linked context

Closes #91815

Related: SecretRef-backed Google Chat/Gateway health diagnostics reported from 2026.6.5.

Requested by a maintainer in issue triage.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: SecretRef-backed `gateway.auth.password` was resolvable to the Gateway process but unavailable to client-side diagnostic commands, causing raw `GatewaySecretRefUnavailableError` or misleading unhealthy/unreachable output.
- Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux runner, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_3d12c04b5fd5`, run `run_3acc3db512f2`, OpenClaw source branch `fix/secretref-diagnostic-gateway-health-91815`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: built the synced source with `OPENCLAW_BUILD_ALL_NO_PNPM=1 node scripts/build-all.mjs gatewayWatch`; started a real Gateway with `gateway.auth.mode=password` and `gateway.auth.password` as an env SecretRef visible only to the Gateway process; ran client commands without that env var.
- Evidence after fix: Crabbox run `run_3acc3db512f2` printed `SECRETREF_GATEWAY_HEALTH_PROOF_PASS` after checking all assertions.
- Observed result after fix: `gateway status --deep --json` succeeded; `health` and `gateway health` exited 1 with `Gateway: reachable` plus the credentials-required diagnostic; `channels status` exited 0 with `Gateway auth unavailable; showing config-only status.`; `browser doctor` exited 1 with a readable SecretRef message; `doctor --allow-exec` exited 0 and noted `Gateway credentials required`; strict `gateway call health --json` still exited 1 with `GatewaySecretRefUnavailableError`.
- What was not tested: Real Google Chat service-account delivery was not needed for this Gateway-auth diagnostic bug; no external channel credentials were used.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Initial Azure Crabbox allocation timed out and was later released (`cbx_fd40cc6a307f`). An earlier AWS attempt hit a remote install hang after `pnpm install` printed `Done`, so the passing proof used the hydrated checkout and direct `node dist/entry.js` after an explicit build.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Current main and shipped `openclaw@2026.6.5` were reproduced earlier with the same split SecretRef setup: `gateway status --deep --json` succeeded while `gateway health`, `browser doctor`, and doctor/channel diagnostic paths surfaced raw or misleading SecretRef failures.

## Tests and validation

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/health.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-health.test.ts src/commands/channels.status.command-flow.test.ts src/commands/channels.config-only-status-output.test.ts extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-manage.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli.coverage.test.ts src/gateway/call.test.ts src/gateway/probe-auth.test.ts extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-shared.test.ts extensions/browser/index.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` - 4 shards passed, 510 tests passed.
- `git diff --check` - passed.
- AWS Crabbox E2E proof `run_3acc3db512f2` - passed with `SECRETREF_GATEWAY_HEALTH_PROOF_PASS`.

Regression coverage added/updated:
- Health command SecretRef-unavailable reachable-Gateway diagnostic.
- Doctor Gateway health SecretRef-unavailable reachable-Gateway diagnostic.
- Channels status auth-unavailable config-only fallback labeling.
- Browser doctor readable SecretRef auth failure.
- Gateway CLI mock contract for the lazy health diagnostic helper.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`

Highest-risk area: Gateway auth/SecretRef handling in diagnostics.

Risk mitigation: Generic `callGateway` credential resolution remains fail-closed; only diagnostic/read-only command surfaces use the existing safe preauth probe to distinguish reachable-but-unauthenticated Gateway state from a real unreachable Gateway. The live proof also verifies strict `gateway call health --json` still fails closed.

## Current review state

Next action: CI watch.

Waiting on: GitHub CI, except `Real behavior proof` per maintainer instruction.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: none yet.

target.title: fix: repair rejected Anthropic thinking replay
target.body:
## Summary

- Repairs persisted signed-thinking replay state after Anthropic/Bedrock rejects a replayed thinking block signature and the stripped retry succeeds.
- Keeps the existing current-turn retry behavior, but now removes the poisoned thinking blocks from the active session branch so subsequent turns do not fail on the same replay.
- Uses the existing transcript rewrite/session update path rather than adding a provider-specific persistent fallback.
- Intentionally out of scope: changing normal signed-thinking preservation when providers accept the replay.

Reviewers should focus on the recovery gate in `thinking.ts`, the active-branch rewrite helper, and the runner hook that refreshes in-memory session state after repair.

## Linked context

Closes #91983

Related: maintainer-requested investigation and live Crabbox reproduction of invalid Anthropic thinking-signature replay.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: invalid persisted Anthropic signed-thinking replay could recover the current turn via stripped retry, but leave the bad thinking block in session history and poison later turns.
- Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux runner `cbx_310989ad2e4d` with live Anthropic `claude-sonnet-4-6` API call.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran an ad hoc Crabbox script that captured a real signed-thinking assistant message, corrupted its `thinkingSignature`, reproduced provider rejection, then exercised `wrapAnthropicStreamWithRecovery` plus `repairRejectedThinkingReplayInSessionManager` and replayed the repaired transcript again.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
LIVE_CAPTURE model=claude-sonnet-4-6 thinkingChars=109 signatureChars=420
REGRESSION_REPRO stopReason=error rejectedInvalidThinkingSignature=true
[agent/embedded] [session-recovery] Anthropic thinking stream error; retrying once without thinking blocks: sessionId=issue-91983-live
[agent/embedded] [session-recovery] stripped thinking blocks after provider rejected replay: repaired=1 sessionKey=unknown
FIX_PROOF stopReason=stop recoveryCalls=1 repairedCount=1 durableThinkingBlocks=0 nextTurnStopReason=stop
run details provider=aws lease=cbx_310989ad2e4d slug=blue-prawn run=run_ccdc1a7188ea type=c7a.8xlarge
```

- Observed result after fix: the corrupted replay failed before repair, the wrapper retried successfully after stripping thinking blocks, one persisted session entry was repaired, no thinking blocks remained in the durable branch, and a subsequent live replay turn succeeded.
- What was not tested: full CI matrix was not complete at PR creation time.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: live proof uses a short synthetic transcript to avoid exposing private user data; the Anthropic API key was injected through 1Password/Crabbox secret forwarding and not printed.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): `REGRESSION_REPRO stopReason=error rejectedInvalidThinkingSignature=true` from the same live proof script.

## Tests and validation

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
  - Final result: `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.
  - Earlier accepted findings fixed: propagated the recovery hook into request-local metadata; gated durable repair on non-error/non-aborted retry results.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/thinking-replay-repair.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/transcript-rewrite.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - Passed: 51 tests.
- Live Crabbox proof above.

Regression coverage added:
- recovery notification fires only after successful stripped retry,
- recovery notification does not fire on thrown retry failure,
- recovery notification does not fire on stream-level retry error result,
- transcript repair strips active-branch thinking blocks, preserves visible text and suffix entries, and skips no-op sessions.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`): Yes

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No

What is the highest-risk area?

Persisted session branch rewrite after a provider replay rejection.

How is that risk mitigated?

Repair only runs after the thinking-signature recovery path and only after the stripped retry returns a successful assistant result. It uses the existing transcript rewrite helper and preserves visible assistant content, falling back to the existing omitted-reasoning placeholder for thinking-only turns.

## Current review state

Next action: watch CI and fix any failures related to this branch.

Still waiting on: CI.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? None yet.

target.title: fix(update): hand off Linux service auto-updates
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91823.

Linux gateway services installed with the older generic OpenClaw service markers can miss systemd-specific env hints during startup auto-update. That made startup auto-update run `openclaw update` as a direct child of the live gateway process, where the update command correctly refuses to mutate the active gateway service process.

This keeps Linux marker-only supervisor detection opt-in and applies it only to update-specific paths:

- startup auto-update now routes marker-only Linux OpenClaw gateway services through managed systemd handoff
- update respawn now uses the same opt-in classification so the post-update restart path stays consistent
- generic restart and default supervisor detection continue to ignore marker-only Linux service env

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/supervisor-markers.test.ts src/infra/update-startup.test.ts src/infra/process-respawn.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
  - first run found a valid consistency issue between startup handoff and update respawn
  - final run: `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Linux startup auto-update failed under a real user systemd gateway service when the service env had only stale generic OpenClaw service markers and no systemd-specific hints.

Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox, real user systemd service, packed OpenClaw package installed into a temporary npm prefix.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built and packed the branch, installed the tarball, created `openclaw-gateway.service` with `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER=openclaw`, `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND=gateway`, and `UnsetEnvironment=OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT INVOCATION_ID SYSTEMD_EXEC_PID JOURNAL_STREAM`, then started the gateway with auto-update enabled.

Evidence after fix: Testbox-through-Crabbox provider `blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ktwdrz6dq71amtvgk027qxch`, Actions run `27382176713`; output included `patch_present=1`, `health={"ok":true,"status":"live"}`, and `auto-update handoff started`.

Observed result after fix: The stale marker-only Linux service started managed update handoff and did not log `auto-update attempt failed`.

What was not tested: A full package update to completion was not allowed to mutate a persistent user install; the proof verifies the broken startup decision and handoff routing in an isolated packed-install service.

## Regression repro

Unpatched `origin/main` was rebuilt and tested with the same stale service shape.

- Testbox-through-Crabbox provider `blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ktwec4jjmhaf9em2xp2w1tr6`, Actions run `27382620484`
- output included `patch_present=0`, `health={"ok":true,"status":"live"}`, `auto-update attempt failed`, and manual `openclaw update` exited `1` with `Package updates cannot run from inside the gateway service process.`

target.title: fix(agents): classify structured unsupported model errors
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve structured provider error details for failover classification without changing stored/displayed `errorMessage`.
- Classify OpenAI-compatible `Not supported model <model-id>` details as `model_not_found` instead of schema/tool-payload failures.
- Add typed assistant error metadata and full-path regression coverage for thrown SDK-shaped errors, assistant `errorBody`, user-facing payloads, and OpenAI-compatible transport loopback.

Closes #92118

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenAI-compatible providers can return HTTP 400 bodies where `error.message` is `Param Incorrect` while `error.param` contains `Not supported model <model-id>`. OpenClaw now classifies that as `model_not_found` and avoids misleading schema/tool-payload copy.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux lease `cbx_f55aeccc0a97` (`c7a.8xlarge`), run `run_de58983b891d`, using the dirty branch sync with a loopback OpenAI-compatible HTTP server returning the exact reported payload.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Crabbox script started a local `/v1/chat/completions` loopback server returning `{"error":{"code":"400","message":"Param Incorrect","param":"Not supported model some-model-id"}}`, then called `createOpenAICompletionsTransportStreamFn()` and checked `resolveFailoverReasonFromError()`, `classifyAssistantFailoverReason()`, and `formatUserFacingAssistantErrorText()`.

Evidence after fix: The Crabbox proof printed `OPENCLAW_92118_FIXED_PROOF` with `dependencyShapeClassification.reason: "model_not_found"`, `classifications.assistantFailoverReason: "model_not_found"`, and user-facing text `The selected model was not found by the provider. Check the model id or choose a different model.`

Observed result after fix: The exact reported payload no longer falls through to schema/tool-payload copy.

What was not tested: A live third-party Xiaomi/MiFY credentialed provider call was not run; the proof uses a local OpenAI-compatible loopback server with the exact reported response body and real OpenClaw OpenAI-compatible transport.

## Verification

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (ran twice after formatting; final result: clean, no accepted/actionable findings)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/live-model-errors.test.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.isbillingerrormessage.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.formatassistanterrortext.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` (2 shards, 616 tests passed)
- `../crabbox/bin/crabbox run --provider aws ...` (`run_de58983b891d`, `cbx_f55aeccc0a97`) for the reported loopback repro/fix proof
- `git diff --check`

target.body:
## Summary

- fix OTLP log correlation for OpenClaw-owned logger records emitted inside active diagnostic trace scopes
- keep explicit logger-supplied trace objects untrusted so plugin/user-provided trace IDs cannot spoof OTLP ancestry
- extend the OTEL QA smoke to fail unless exported log records include trace/span correlation IDs while default log bodies remain placeholders

Fixes #91865 for the trace/span correlation portion. This intentionally does not change default OTLP log body export; log bodies remain gated by content capture.

## Verification

This teaches provider auth resolution to bridge managed/non-env SecretRef provider `apiKey` entries through the active runtime config snapshot.
