target.title: fix: handle Claude CLI synthetic placeholders
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90789 by recognizing Claude Code CLI synthetic assistant placeholders as CLI-generated no-output metadata instead of treating the turn as an ordinary empty result.

This covers the observed `model: "<synthetic>"` assistant events with:

- `No response requested.`
- `Continue from where you left off.`

## What changed

- Detect Claude stream-json assistant records where `message.model === "<synthetic>"` and the text exactly matches a known Claude CLI placeholder.
- Preserve a `syntheticPlaceholder` marker across both buffered JSONL parsing and streaming parsing, including when a later empty `type: "result"` event arrives.
- Propagate that marker into embedded run metadata as `terminalReplyKind: "synthetic-placeholder"`.
- **Make the classifier path reachable from `runPreparedCliAgent`**: `executeCliAttempt` previously threw `FailoverError(empty_response)` on every empty-text turn, swallowing the synthetic marker before `buildCliRunResult` could surface it. The throw is now skipped when `output.syntheticPlaceholder === true`, so the run result keeps `terminalReplyKind` and the classifier returns the richer `empty_result` verdict.
- Classify synthetic-placeholder terminal replies as fallback-worthy `empty_result` for model fallback / observability, including non-GPT models such as `claude-cli/Claude-Opus-4.7`.
- Keep explicit OpenClaw silent replies (`NO_REPLY`) out of fallback behavior.

## Rationale

The placeholder is generated by the CLI/runtime, not by the assistant and not by OpenClaw's explicit silent-reply contract. Surfacing it as a normal reply would be wrong, but silently swallowing it can leave Telegram/group-chat turns with no visible message and no useful recovery path. Treating it as an empty-result classification preserves observability and allows existing fallback handling to recover.

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-output.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts`
  - 4 files passed, 50 tests passed
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/cli-output.ts src/agents/cli-output.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.ts src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/types.ts`
  - 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `pnpm exec oxfmt src/agents/cli-runner.ts src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts`
  - clean
- `git diff --check`
  - clean

New `src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts` drives `runPreparedCliAgent` end-to-end with a mocked CLI output carrying `syntheticPlaceholder: true`, asserting both that `terminalReplyKind` reaches the result metadata and that `classifyEmbeddedAgentRunResultForModelFallback` emits the `empty_result` verdict — the integration coverage ClawSweeper P1 asked for.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Claude Code CLI emits a `<synthetic>` assistant placeholder (`No response requested.` / `Continue from where you left off.`) followed by an empty `type: "result"` event when its loop is wedged/timed-out. Before this fix, OpenClaw's `executeCliAttempt` saw the empty text and threw a generic `FailoverError(empty_response)` which left no `terminalReplyKind` metadata and no synthetic-placeholder signal for the model-fallback classifier, so the affected Telegram turn went silent with no recovery path (issue #90789).
- **Real environment tested**: local OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/wanglu241/code/openclaw`, branch `fix/90789-claude-cli-synthetic-placeholder` (head `8d6f1a7a`), node v26.0.0, pnpm/tsx 4.22.3, macOS 25.4.0 arm64. Parser + classifier executed against the real source via `pnpm exec tsx`, with the exact JSONL byte sequence the CLI emits (taken from `src/agents/cli-output.test.ts`'s captured fixture).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Build a one-off harness (`scratch/synthetic-placeholder-proof.ts`) that calls `parseCliJsonl(...)` with the synthetic-placeholder stream-json sequence and then invokes `classifyEmbeddedAgentRunResultForModelFallback(...)` against the resulting run-result shape.
  2. Check out the upstream/main versions of `src/agents/cli-output.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.ts`, and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/types.ts`; run the harness; record output.
  3. Restore the PR head versions of those files; run the harness again; record output.
  4. Restore working tree, run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts` to drive the same flow through `runPreparedCliAgent` end-to-end.
- **Evidence after fix:** redacted terminal output from `pnpm exec tsx scratch/synthetic-placeholder-proof.ts`:

```text
=== BASELINE: upstream/main (no synthetic placeholder handling) ===
---- parser output ----
{
  "text": "",
  "sessionId": "redacted-session",
  "usage": {
    "input": 8
  }
}
---- classifier verdict ----
null

=== CANDIDATE: PR fix/90789-claude-cli-synthetic-placeholder ===
---- parser output ----
{
  "text": "",
  "sessionId": "redacted-session",
  "usage": {
    "input": 8
  },
  "syntheticPlaceholder": true
}
---- classifier verdict ----
{
  "message": "claude-cli/Claude-Opus-4.7 ended with a synthetic placeholder and no visible assistant reply",
  "reason": "format",
  "code": "empty_result"
}
```

End-to-end `runPreparedCliAgent` integration test live output (`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts`):

```text
 RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/wanglu241/code/openclaw

 ✓ |agents| src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts (2 tests) 3.04s
   ✓ runPreparedCliAgent synthetic-placeholder propagation > marks runs with synthetic-placeholder terminal reply kind and triggers empty_result fallback
   ✓ runPreparedCliAgent synthetic-placeholder propagation > does not mark ordinary CLI runs and leaves the classifier null

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed (2)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: with the PR applied, the JSONL parser surfaces `syntheticPlaceholder: true` (baseline does not), the run result carries `terminalReplyKind: "synthetic-placeholder"` (baseline strips it), and the model-fallback classifier returns `{ reason: "format", code: "empty_result", message: "...synthetic placeholder..." }` (baseline returns `null`). Downstream model-fallback machinery therefore now sees a fallback-worthy outcome instead of a silent turn for the wedge-aborted Claude CLI case from #90789.
- **What was not tested**: Native Telegram Desktop capture under Crabbox could not exercise this code path because the bundled `extensions/qa-lab` mock provider does not (yet) emit `<synthetic>` stream-json events to OpenClaw — that is why the workflow's `Run agentic native Telegram proof` job posted a `comparison.status: skipped, pass: false` capture-infrastructure manifest. Adding mock-provider support for synthetic-placeholder injection is tracked as an out-of-scope follow-up; the live tsx run above and the new `runPreparedCliAgent` integration test cover the same code path end-to-end at the runtime layer.

## Out-of-scope follow-ups

- Teach `extensions/qa-lab` mock providers to optionally emit Claude CLI `<synthetic>` placeholder stream-json so the Mantis Telegram Desktop scenario can produce a native before/after recording for this fix.

target.title: fix: surface safe terminal tool fallbacks
target.body:
## Summary

- Add opt-in terminal fallback metadata through agent-core, session tool definitions, subscribe handling, and the embedded runner.
- Surface safe terminal summaries for tool-loop and incomplete-turn exits, while keeping undeclared tool output neutral instead of leaking raw results.
- Declare structured fallbacks for `cron` and `web_fetch`, and keep Docker image builds from carrying stale `dist` output.
- Tighten delivery/side-effect evidence for channel replies, source-reply deliveries, context-engine pairing, compaction ownership, heartbeat lifecycle handling, and tool-plugin result preservation.

## Contract note

- `terminalSummary` and `terminalResultFallback` are intentionally public tool metadata for safe terminal presentation.
- Tools opt in explicitly; undeclared raw tool output is not surfaced as a fallback.
- Generic tool activity, accepted child-session spawns, messaging sends, cron additions, and replay-invalid aborts continue to suppress fallback model replay so side effects are not duplicated.
- `defineToolPlugin` preserves intentional helper-created tool results while continuing to JSON-wrap bare text-shaped objects with `content` and `details` fields.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/tool-plugin.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `pnpm check:docs`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=gpt-5.5 --thinking codex=high`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Channel-facing agents that ended a turn after successful tool work could reply with planning text, repeat promises, or expose unsafe raw fallback text instead of delivering the completed tool result.

Real environment tested: A live Discord-backed OpenClaw stack using the patched image and a connected bot. Identifying stack, bot, channel, image, and run IDs are intentionally omitted from this public PR.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Sent live prompts that exercised a scheduler/cron path and a `web_fetch` tool-loop timeout path through the running gateway.

Evidence after fix:

Redacted live channel output, scheduler path:

```text
The requested scheduler state cannot be checked from this runtime because no scheduler, cron, gateway, or exec tool is available in this channel session. I cannot claim a live cron check without that tool.
```

Redacted live channel output, `web_fetch` terminal fallback path:

```text
URL: https://example.com
Final URL: https://example.com
Status: 200
Title: Example Domain
Cached: true
Truncated: false
```

Observed result after fix: The channel received user-visible results for both proof cases. The `web_fetch` fallback included URL, final URL, status, title, cache state, and truncation state, with no raw unsafe tool dump. The scheduler path returned a direct capability blocker instead of a promise to perform an unavailable check.

What was not tested: Broad cross-channel E2E beyond Discord was not rerun. One unrelated live runtime metadata drift was observed during proof and is omitted here because it is not part of this patch.

target.title: security: add Security Matrix runtime-fact audit model
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a Security Matrix runtime-fact evaluator.
- Models actor, influence source, tool capability, approval state, and operator policy as separate inputs.
- Adds default policy data, a deterministic evaluator, tool capability resolution, an audit-event object builder, tests, and documentation.
- Adds a small before-tool-call fact adapter so future runtime wiring can build the same audit event shape from explicit boundary facts without changing execution behavior.
- Hardens custom policy parsing so malformed decisions cannot weaken the default policy.

## Scope

This PR remains non-enforcing and observe-only.

It does not block tool calls, require confirmation at runtime, emit runtime diagnostic events, change UI, change plugin behavior, or alter runtime tool execution. The before-tool-call adapter only builds an audit event object and exposes an explicit opt-in config predicate for future wiring.

## Validation

Validated locally at PR head `d192028ab6cd7f962a7007f0f94c77d6b8b8e4aa`.

Commands run:

```bash
pnpm test src/security/security-matrix/evaluate.test.ts src/agents/security-matrix-before-call.test.ts
pnpm changed:lanes --base upstream/main --json
pnpm exec oxlint src/security/security-matrix/ src/agents/security-matrix-before-call.test.ts
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/security/security-matrix.md
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
```

Results:

- Focused tests passed: `src/security/security-matrix/evaluate.test.ts` and `src/agents/security-matrix-before-call.test.ts`
- `Test Files 2 passed (2)`
- `Tests 28 passed (28)`
- changed-lanes reported `core=true`, `coreTests=true`, `docs=true`, `tooling=true`
- changed-lanes reported `extensions=false`, `extensionTests=false`, `apps=false`, `liveDockerTooling=false`, `releaseMetadata=false`, `all=false`
- oxlint completed with no reported errors

## Real behavior proof

The before-tool-call adapter builds the same audit-event shape from explicit boundary facts without emitting, blocking, or mutating execution. target.title: Feature Request: Add workboard card delete/remove API
target.body:
**Current limitation:**
The Workboard plugin currently provides no way to permanently delete or remove a card. The available lifecycle actions are limited to:
- `create`, `claim`, `release`, `complete`, `block`, `unblock`, `reclaim`, `reassign`
- There is no `delete` or `remove` operation exposed through the tool API or the management UI.

**Impact:**
- Archived cards remain in the database and accumulate over time.
- Users who want to clean up stale/abandoned cards cannot do so without manual database intervention.
- The management UI does not have a "delete" button.

**Requested feature:**
1. Add a `delete` or `remove` action to the Workboard card API (matching the existing tool schema pattern).
2. Optionally expose a delete button in the Workboard management UI for archived/completed cards.
3. Consider soft-delete with a garbage-collect period, or a hard-delete with confirmation, depending on the project’s data-retention policy.

**Environment:**
- OpenClaw version: 2026.6.5
- Plugin: workboard

Thank you for considering this.

target.title: fix(cron): skip startup catch-up slots that predate schedule updates
target.body:
## Summary

When `cron.update` changes a cron job's schedule (e.g., monthly day 10 → day 11), a subsequent gateway restart can incorrectly classify the newly-computed "previous" slot as missed — triggering the job at the wrong time.

**Root cause:** The restart catch-up logic in `isRunnableJob()` computes `previousRunAtMs` from the current cron expression and compares it against `lastRunAtMs`. After a schedule change, the new expression's previous slot may fall between the last real run and the update time — a slot that was never intended to execute. Without a timestamp anchor, the catch-up logic cannot distinguish genuine missed slots from schedule-change artifacts.

## Fix

Record `scheduleUpdatedAtMs` on `CronJobState` when `cron.update` changes the schedule or enabled state. During restart catch-up, skip any candidate slot whose `previousRunAtMs` falls before `scheduleUpdatedAtMs` — the slot predates the update and is not truly missed.

**Two guard points** (both must be protected):
1. `isRunnableJob()` — the primary missed-slot classifier (startup catch-up)
2. `deferPendingBackoffMissedCronSlots()` — the error-backoff deferral path

## Files changed

- `src/cron/types.ts`: +5 (new `scheduleUpdatedAtMs` field on `CronJobState`)
- `src/cron/service/ops.ts`: +1 (record timestamp on schedule/enabled change)
- `src/cron/service/timer.ts`: +25/-1 (guard in both catch-up paths)
- `src/cron/service/timer.issue-91944-cron-update-catchup.test.ts`: +310 (5 regression test cases)
- `scripts/proof-91944-cron-catchup.mjs`: +50 (standalone proof script)

## Related

Fixes #91944

Previous attempts: #92250, #92277 (same fix direction, closed due to ClawSweeper transport failures — not code rejection)

### Root cause analysis

```
cron.update (ops.ts)
  → updates schedule expr (day 10 → day 11)
  → recomputes nextRunAtMs correctly (June 11)
  → [BUG: no timestamp recorded for the change]

gateway restart
  → planStartupCatchup()
    → collectRunnableJobs(allowCronMissedRunByLastRun: true)
      → isRunnableJob()
        → nowMs < nextRunAtMs (correct, in future) → skip line 1049
        → allowCronMissedRunByLastRun=true → enter missed-slot check
        → computeJobPreviousRunAtMs(new expr) = May 11 15:18
        → previousRunAtMs (May 11) > lastRunAtMs (May 10) → MISFIRED!
```

The fix adds a timestamp anchor that gates both missed-slot inference paths:
- `previousRunAtMs < scheduleUpdatedAtMs` → skip (slot predates update)
- `previousRunAtMs >= scheduleUpdatedAtMs` → normal catch-up (genuine missed slot)

### Comparison with sibling implementations

The fix follows the same pattern as `updatedAtMs` on the job record — a single timestamp set at the mutation point and consulted at decision points. Both catch-up paths (`isRunnableJob` and `deferPendingBackoffMissedCronSlots`) are protected symmetrically, matching the existing pattern where both paths already share the `computeJobPreviousRunAtMs` / `lastRunAtMs` comparison.

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Cron job with updated schedule incorrectly fires on gateway restart when a pre-update computed slot is misclassified as "missed"
- **Real environment tested**: Linux Node 24 (local reproduction of the exact reporter timeline from issue #91944)
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  $ node --import tsx scripts/proof-91944-cron-catchup.mjs
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```console
  $ node --import tsx scripts/proof-91944-cron-catchup.mjs
  ============================================================
  Proof: #91944 cron update catch-up guard
  ============================================================

  Reporter timeline (from issue #91944):
    1. Old schedule:  18 15 10 * * (monthly day 10)
    2. Last run:      2026-05-10T15:18:00Z
    3. API update:    day 10 → day 11  (2026-06-09T22:30:00Z)
    4. Restart:       2026-06-10T12:33:00Z (double restart)

  Without fix:
    computeJobPreviousRunAtMs(new expr) = 2026-05-11T15:18:00Z
    previousRunAtMs (May 11) > lastRunAtMs (May 10)
    → BUG: slot classified as missed → job fires incorrectly

  With fix (scheduleUpdatedAtMs guard):
    scheduleUpdatedAtMs = 2026-06-09T22:30:00Z (update time)
    previousRunAtMs (May 11) < scheduleUpdatedAtMs (June 9)
    → SKIP: slot predates the schedule update

  Fix surface:
    src/cron/types.ts        +5  (scheduleUpdatedAtMs field)
    src/cron/service/ops.ts  +1  (record on schedule/enabled change)
    src/cron/service/timer.ts +24 (guard in both catch-up paths)

  5 behavior scenarios verified:
    1. Slot predating schedule update → correctly skipped
    2. Genuine missed slot (no update) → still fires
    3. Backoff slot predating update → correctly skipped
    4. Legacy jobs (no scheduleUpdatedAtMs) → unchanged
    5. Slot postdating update → still fires

  80 existing cron scenarios all green, zero regressions.
  ============================================================
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: Jobs with `scheduleUpdatedAtMs` set skip pre-update slots during restart catch-up. Jobs without the field (legacy) retain existing behavior. Genuinely missed slots after the update still fire correctly. The exact reporter timeline (monthly day-10 → day-11, double restart at day 10) is reproduced and verified.
- **What was not tested**: Full gateway launchd double-restart upgrade cycle with live Telegram delivery (requires a production OpenClaw gateway); the in-process restart simulation covers the same code paths exercised by the cron service timer.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: Feat/discord slash command deploy 75888
target.body:
## Summary
Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:
If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.
- **Problem:** Discord native slash-command deploy lacked a configurable lifecycle policy (`always` / `changed-only` / `disabled`), durable fingerprints across gateway restarts for `changed-only`, safe in-process coordination when multiple Discord accounts trigger background slash deploy from one gateway. Separately: Vitest 4 removed **`TestRunner`** from **`vitest`**, breaking the repo custom non-isolated runner; Discord provider harness mocks needed the new **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** export.
- **Why it matters:** Fewer redundant Discord application-command REST calls when unchanged (rate-limit / churn), explicit opt-out (`disabled`), restart-stable `changed-only` semantics, and predictable multi-account deploy ordering; tests must boot on current Vitest.
- **What changed (product/code):** `channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy` accepts string shorthand **or** `{ mode }` (default **`changed-only`** when omitted); merge persist successful deploy fingerprints; preload deployer snapshot for `changed-only`; queue per-process background deploy; docs (`docs/channels/discord.md`), changelog, config UI hints, core types + zod schema + plugin-sdk runtime helper alignment, regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`, new/updated Discord extension tests around deploy state and wiring.
- **What changed (test infra):** `test/non-isolated-runner.ts` extends **`VitestTestRunner`** from **`vitest/runners`**; `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts` mocks **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** for `monitor/commands.js` partial mock compatibility.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Non-Discord channels/providers; unrelated gateway loops beyond shared config/schema surfaces required for the new key.
_AI-assisted._ Local verification: targeted Discord extension Vitest shard (see **Repro + Verification → Actual**).
## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra
## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra
## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/75888
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression (issue-tracked behavior gaps for Discord slash deploy policy + persistence + cleanup)
## Root Cause (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.
- **Root cause:** N/A for a single accidental line bug—this PR implements the missing Discord deploy policy + persistence model described in #75888, plus modernizes the custom Vitest runner for Vitest 4 exports and updates incomplete module mocks after adding new command helpers.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No durable fingerprint + explicit deploy policy previously; tests did not cover the new export on a partially mocked `monitor/commands.js` module.
- **Contributing context (if known):** #75888 requested configurable deploy strategy and cross-restart fingerprinting with safe defaults and cleanup semantics.
## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts`, plus harness `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts`.
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** `slashCommandDeploy` parse/default; fingerprint merge semantics; provider monitor wiring with mocked command helpers including deploy resolver; schema acceptance for new config shape.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** Exercises contracts without live Discord credentials.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** N/A beyond files above.
- **If no new test is added, why not:** N/A
## User-visible / Behavior Changes
Optional **`channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy`**: **`"always" | "changed-only" | "disabled"`** **or** **`{ mode: ... }`**. Default when omitted: **`changed-only`**. **`changed-only`** persists successful deploy fingerprints across restarts to skip redundant REST deploy when command definitions are unchanged. Multi-account Discord background slash deploy is serialized per gateway process. Docs/changelog describe behavior; config baseline hash regenerated for schema/docs drift checks.
Docs hub (sync lag may apply): https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/discord
## Diagram (if applicable)
For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.
```text
N/A
```
## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **Yes**
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Writes/reads local gateway persistent state for non-secret Discord slash deploy fingerprint metadata (keyed per Discord application/account pairing as implemented).
## Repro + Verification
### Environment
- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord extension Vitest project (`extension-discord`) — no live Discord token required for this command
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A
### Steps
1. From the OpenClaw repository root, ensure dependencies are installed (`pnpm install`).
2. Run:
```
pnpm test \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \
  -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tee proof-discord-verbose.txt
```
### Expected
The command exits **0**. Vitest reports **7** passing test files and **127** passing tests for the `extension-discord` shard (see **Actual**).
### Actual
```
> openclaw@2026.6.1 test
> node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts

  RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/liminglin/Funny/openclaw

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  127 passed (127)
   Start at  15:38:27
   Duration  5.65s (transform 3.58s, setup 286ms, import 4.32s, tests 921ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 8.54s
```
Process exit code: **0**
## Evidence
Attach at least one:
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (Vitest 4 runner bootstrap fixed; missing mock export fixed; green shard output above)
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)
## Human Verification (required)
What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:
- **Verified scenarios:** Ran the exact **`pnpm test …`** command in **Steps**; confirmed **Actual** summary matches terminal (**7** files, **127** tests, exit **0**); reviewed `slashCommandDeploy` docs + schema defaults for consistency with parser.
- **Edge cases checked:** `disabled`, `always`, `changed-only`; multi-account queue assumption at coordinator level.
- **What you did **not** verify:** Live production Discord bot/guild roundtrip beyond extension/unit coverage (additional confidence from PR CI).
## Review Conversations
- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.
## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? **Yes**
- Config/env changes? **Yes**
- Migration needed? **No**
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A (operators who need prior implicit “always redeploy every restart” behavior can set `slashCommandDeploy: "always"` explicitly).
## Risks and Mitigations
- **Risk:** Fleet-wide cold start may still issue full deploys until first successful fingerprint write exists per application/account pairing.
  - **Mitigation:** `changed-only` amortizes after first success; `disabled` / `always` explicit operator posture.

## Real behavior proof

  - Behavior addressed:
    Discord `slashCommandDeploy=changed-only` persists deploy fingerprints in Discord plugin state (SQLite) and skips redundant Discord application-command REST on gateway restart when the serialized command set is unchanged.

  - Real environment tested:
    macOS (darwin arm64), Node v24.14.0; **live Discord gateway** on branch `feat/discord-slash-command-deploy-75888` @ **`7bf4b50`** (`OpenClaw 2026.6.1` local build), guild `1471499665584296039`, application `1471498116237885474`, `--profile discord-proof`, token via `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` env (not pasted in PR).

  - Exact steps or command run after this patch:
    1. `pnpm build`
    2. `source ~/.env/third-party-apis.zsh && export OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-discord-proof`
    3. **Prep (cold fingerprint):** `sqlite3 ~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite "DELETE FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE plugin_id='discord' AND namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"`
    4. **RUN1:** `node openclaw.mjs --log-level debug --profile discord-proof gateway run --verbose --port 19099 --force` — wait for `deploy-commands:done`
    5. Stop gateway; verify plugin-state fingerprint row exists (see Evidence)
    6. **RUN2:** rerun the same gateway command unchanged — wait for `deploy-commands:done`

  - Evidence after fix:
    Terminal transcript (`pnpm test … --reporter=verbose`) ends with `Test Files 7 passed`, `Tests 127 passed`, exit **0**.

    **Live gateway @ `16ddd7f2` (local transcript, token redacted):**
    - **Startup migration (first boot on this head with legacy JSON present):** gateway auto-migrated `discord/slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` → plugin state and archived legacy source to `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json.migrated`.
    - **RUN1 (empty plugin-state fingerprint, commands already registered on Discord):** `deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47`; `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get` to `/applications/1471498116237885474/commands`; `deploy-commands:done` ~4.8s; fingerprint persisted in plugin state:
      `sqlite3 … "SELECT entry_key, value_json FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"` → `1471498116237885474:default` with `global:reconcile` SHA256 `0adc06bf…9297aa9`.
    - **RUN2 (unchanged config/commands):** `deploy-commands:schedule` + `deploy-commands:done` ~1.6s; **zero** `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:*` lines (REST deploy skipped).

    Key log lines (RUN1):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:start … path=/applications/1471498116237885474/commands
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:done … requestMs=715
deploy-commands:done … ~4792ms
```

    Key log lines (RUN2):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
deploy-commands:done … ~1659ms
(no native-slash-command-deploy-rest:* lines)
```

  - Observed result after fix:
    - `changed-only` reads/writes fingerprints in **Discord plugin state** (`openKeyedStore`, namespace `slash-command-deploy-hashes`, key `applicationId:accountId`) under `~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite`.
    - After fingerprint exists, immediate gateway restart skips Discord deploy REST while still logging `deploy-commands:scheduled` / `deploy-commands:done`.
    - Legacy JSON is migrated via startup/doctor path only; runtime steady-state does not read the JSON sidecar.

  - What was not tested:
    - `slashCommandDeploy: "disabled"` on live gateway restart
    - Live Discord 429 / `retry_after` under rate limit
    - Multi-account serial deploy queue under live dual-bot startup

  - Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
    Prior behavior: every gateway restart unconditionally reconciled slash commands via Discord REST even when the command list was unchanged.



---

## Migration proof (commits b3282460 + 16ddd7f2)

**What was added after original live proof:**

Two follow-up commits resolve the ClawSweeper P1 finding: shipped `discord/command-deploy-cache.json` (`{ hashes }` schema, no appId/accountId) was not migrated to plugin state on upgrade.

**Fix:**
- `slash-command-deploy-state.ts`: added `DISCORD_COMMAND_DEPLOY_CACHE_LEGACY_JSON_RELATIVE_PATH` constant
- `model-picker-preferences-migrations.ts`: new migration plan reads `discord/command-deploy-cache.json`, calls `mergeDiscordAccountConfig` per account to resolve both root-inherited and per-account `applicationId`, then writes one plugin-state entry per `applicationId:accountId` pair. `shouldReplaceExistingEntry: () => false` prevents overwriting a fresher `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` migration.


**Migration scenarios covered by new tests:**
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with root `applicationId` → single `appId:default` key
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with no `applicationId` → empty entries (graceful cold start)
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with two accounts each having distinct per-account `applicationId` → two keys `111100001111:bot1` and `222200002222:bot2`

**Doctor upgrade proof (current-head `7bf4b50`, Node v22.19.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.1):**

**Combined proof:**
- Doctor --fix migrates v2026.5.28 shipped `command-deploy-cache.json` → per-account plugin state entries (`applicationId:accountId` key format) with correct hash passthrough and proper archival of the legacy source.

target.title: security: add Security Matrix runtime-fact audit model
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a Security Matrix runtime-fact evaluator.
- Models actor, influence source, tool capability, approval state, and operator policy as separate inputs.
- Adds default policy data, a deterministic evaluator, tool capability resolution, an audit-event object builder, tests, and documentation.
- Adds a small before-tool-call fact adapter so future runtime wiring can build the same audit event shape from explicit boundary facts without changing execution behavior.
- Hardens custom policy parsing so malformed decisions cannot weaken the default policy.

## Scope

This PR remains non-enforcing and observe-only.

It does not block tool calls, require confirmation at runtime, emit runtime diagnostic events, change UI, change plugin behavior, or alter runtime tool execution. The before-tool-call adapter only builds an audit event object and exposes an explicit opt-in config predicate for future wiring.

## Validation

Validated locally at PR head `d192028ab6cd7f962a7007f0f94c77d6b8b8e4aa`.

Commands run:

```bash
pnpm test src/security/security-matrix/evaluate.test.ts src/agents/security-matrix-before-call.test.ts
pnpm changed:lanes --base upstream/main --json
pnpm exec oxlint src/security/security-matrix/ src/agents/security-matrix-before-call.test.ts
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/security/security-matrix.md
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
```

Results:

- Focused tests passed: `src/security/security-matrix/evaluate.test.ts` and `src/agents/security-matrix-before-call.test.ts`
- `Test Files 2 passed (2)`
- `Tests 28 passed (28)`
- changed-lanes reported `core=true`, `coreTests=true`, `docs=true`, `tooling=true`
- changed-lanes reported `extensions=false`, `extensionTests=false`, `apps=false`, `liveDockerTooling=false`, `releaseMetadata=false`, `all=false`
- oxlint completed with no reported errors
- docs formatting reported `Docs formatting clean (663 files).`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` reported no whitespace errors

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Security Matrix runtime-fact evaluation now produces deterministic audit decisions from actor, influence source, capability, approval state, and operator policy. The before-tool-call adapter builds the same audit-event shape from explicit boundary facts without emitting, blocking, or mutating execution. Malformed custom policy decisions are ignored instead of being treated as policy output.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux checkout at `~/projects/openclaw`, branch `security/matrix-audit-model`, PR head `d192028ab6cd7f962a7007f0f94c77d6b8b8e4aa`, Node v24.15.0 via the repository pnpm environment.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Ran focused tests through the repository `pnpm test` wrapper.
  2. Ran changed-lane detection, targeted oxlint, docs formatting, and whitespace checks.
  3. Added regression coverage for malformed custom policy decisions shaped as both an object and a string.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  28 passed (28)
Docs formatting clean (663 files).
```

- Observed result after fix: The evaluator keeps the default `web_fetch` + `exec` block decision when custom policy data contains invalid decision values such as `permit`, even when `allowPolicyWeakening` is enabled. Valid policy decisions still resolve through the existing deterministic rule path.
- What was not tested: Runtime hook emission, runtime enforcement, strict config schema adoption, CLI display, plugin UI, and live tool execution are intentionally not changed by this PR.

target.title: fix: split image setup and request timeout semantics
target.body:
## Summary
- split image-description timeout handling so setup and provider request both use the configured `tools.media.image.timeoutSeconds` budget instead of shrinking the request budget by elapsed setup time
- preserve the full configured timeout for the provider request instead of silently subtracting setup latency down to a tiny remaining budget
- add focused tests covering request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and slow-setup request behavior
- keep public docs user-facing: the image timeout still applies to the request itself and is not reduced by earlier preparation work
- keep the heavier image-tool latency and runtime-loading reductions for follow-up PRs after this timeout-semantics fix

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider docker --docker-image node:22-bookworm --timing-json --shell -- "bash -lc 'corepack enable >/dev/null 2>&1 && env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 corepack pnpm test src/media-understanding/image.test.ts'"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: image timeout errors no longer collapse setup latency into a tiny remaining provider timeout; setup and request failures now report distinct phases.
Real environment tested: Crabbox `docker` provider with `node:22-bookworm`, synced from this worktree.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: run the targeted media-understanding Vitest file via Crabbox Docker using the command above.
Evidence after fix: `src/media-understanding/image.test.ts` passes with 25 tests; assertions cover request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and a slow setup that still passes the full configured timeout through to the provider request.
Observed result after fix: the test suite passed, provider-request calls still received `timeoutMs: 1000` after a mocked `400ms` setup delay, and timeout messages now distinguish `setup` vs `request`.
What was not tested: live provider behavior and the follow-up PRs that will reduce the image runtime-loading latency itself.

target.title: fix(agents): do not misclassify client-disconnect abort as run timeout
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: Issue #90764 reports that embedded agent runs abort at ~165–180 s wall-clock regardless of `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds` or `models.providers.*.timeoutSeconds`. The run exits with "Request timed out before a response was generated." and the message advises increasing `timeoutSeconds`, but increasing it has no effect on the abort timing.
- **Root Cause**: `onAbort` and `onExternalAbortSignal` in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` call `isTimeoutError(reason)` on the abort-signal reason to decide whether to set `timedOut = true`. `isTimeoutError` calls `hasTimeoutHint`, which calls `isTimeoutErrorMessage` — that function matches the pattern `/\boperation was aborted\b/i` against the error message. `AbortController.abort()` with no arguments produces `DOMException("This operation was aborted", "AbortError")`; that message matches the pattern, so `hasTimeoutHint` returns `true` and `isTimeoutError` returns `true` for what is actually a plain client cancellation, setting `timedOut = true` erroneously. `watchClientDisconnect` in `src/gateway/http-common.ts` calls `abortController.abort()` with no args whenever the HTTP socket closes — for example, when a reverse-proxy or keepalive layer evicts the connection after a long period of no SSE output during a thinking phase. This fires `params.abortSignal`, which triggers `onAbort`, which then sets `timedOut = true` with no relation to the configured timeout values.
- **Fix**: Replace `isTimeoutError` with a new `isSignalTimeoutReason` export from `src/agents/failover-error.ts`. `isSignalTimeoutReason` checks only `readErrorName(reason) === "TimeoutError"` — the name produced exclusively by `AbortSignal.timeout()` and `makeTimeoutAbortReason()`, the two intentional timeout-creation paths. Plain `AbortError` (`name = "AbortError"`) is a cancellation and returns `false`. `isTimeoutError` is unchanged for all other callers that classify general LLM error messages, where the Ollama NDJSON / stream-abort patterns still apply.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/agents/failover-error.ts` — export `isSignalTimeoutReason`: checks only `name === "TimeoutError"`, no text-matching heuristic.
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` — replace `isTimeoutError` import and both abort-signal handler call sites (`onExternalAbortSignal` and `onAbort`) with `isSignalTimeoutReason`.
  - `src/agents/failover-error.test.ts` — add `isSignalTimeoutReason` describe block: plain `AbortError` DOMException, ABORT_TIMEOUT_RE `AbortError`, `AbortSignal.timeout()` DOMException, `makeTimeoutAbortReason()`-style Error, null/undefined.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - `isTimeoutError` and its text-matching heuristics are unchanged; the change is scoped to the two abort-signal handler call sites.
  - Config surface unchanged (no schema, defaults, doctor migrations, or `docs/reference/config`).
  - Plugin surface unchanged (no plugin SDK, manifest, `extensions/*` api/runtime-api, registry/loader).
  - Gateway protocol unchanged.

### Reproduction

1. Configure `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds: 600` and `models.providers.custom-api-anthropic-com.timeoutSeconds: 600`.
2. Start a run that completes several tool calls, then enters a thinking phase that produces no SSE output for long enough to trigger the client's HTTP connection idle timeout.
3. The reverse proxy or keepalive layer evicts the idle connection; `watchClientDisconnect` fires `abortController.abort()` with no args.
4. **Before this PR**: `onAbort` calls `isTimeoutError(DOMException("This operation was aborted", "AbortError"))` — `hasTimeoutHint` matches the message via `isTimeoutErrorMessage` (`/\boperation was aborted\b/i`), returns `true`; `timedOut = true`; run exits at ~165–180 s with "Request timed out before a response was generated."; increasing `timeoutSeconds` has no effect.
5. **After this PR**: `isSignalTimeoutReason(DOMException("This operation was aborted", "AbortError"))` checks `readErrorName(reason) === "TimeoutError"` → `false`; `timedOut` is not set; the abort is treated as an external cancellation, not a run timeout.

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed (#90764)**: after the fix, a client-disconnect abort no longer sets `timedOut = true` in the embedded attempt; only abort-signal reasons with `name === "TimeoutError"` (from `AbortSignal.timeout()` or `makeTimeoutAbortReason()`) are treated as run timeouts.

**Real environment tested (Linux, Node 22 — Vitest against the production `isSignalTimeoutReason` and the two production abort-signal handler paths)**: `isSignalTimeoutReason` driven directly with each discriminating input; `onAbort` / `onExternalAbortSignal` call sites exercised via the existing full-runner `attempt.test.ts` suite.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `pnpm test src/agents/failover-error.test.ts`; `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.test.ts`; `pnpm format:check` and `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on the three changed files; `pnpm tsgo`.

**Evidence after fix**:

```
failover-error.test.ts   Tests  88 passed (88)
attempt.test.ts          Tests  121 passed (121)
```

The `isSignalTimeoutReason` describe covers: `DOMException("This operation was aborted", "AbortError")` → `false` (watchClientDisconnect regression case; old `isTimeoutError` returned `true` via `isTimeoutErrorMessage` matching `/\boperation was aborted\b/i`); `Error("request aborted", name="AbortError")` → `false` (old `isTimeoutError` returned `true` via `ABORT_TIMEOUT_RE`); `DOMException("signal timed out", "TimeoutError")` → `true`; `Error("request timed out", name="TimeoutError")` → `true`; null/undefined → `false`.

**Observed result after fix**: `isSignalTimeoutReason(new DOMException("This operation was aborted", "AbortError"))` returns `false`; the abort-signal handlers classify client disconnections as `externalAbort=true / timedOut=false`; the run does not surface "Request timed out" for network-layer connection evictions.

**What was not tested**: live gateway repro with a real reverse-proxy idle eviction end-to-end; the abort-signal handler logic and the `isSignalTimeoutReason` discriminator are covered deterministically against the production functions, but a full network-layer round-trip was not driven.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: `isSignalTimeoutReason` is strictly narrower than `isTimeoutError` for the two affected call sites; a timeout path that passes `params.abortSignal` with an error whose `name` is not `"TimeoutError"` will no longer be classified as a timeout.
- **Mitigation**: both intentional internal timeout-creation paths (`makeTimeoutAbortReason()` and `AbortSignal.timeout()`) produce `name === "TimeoutError"`; `isTimeoutError` is unchanged for all LLM error classification callers where the broader text-matching heuristic is appropriate.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Agent runtime
- [x] Tests

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #90764

target.title: [AI-assisted] Persist agent-run error replies in chat history
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: A started Control UI agent run could surface a terminal provider/runtime error live, but `chat.history` after refresh did not retain an assistant error turn for that failed run.
- Solution: Treat agent-run failure replies as real error payloads and make Gateway persist exactly one display-safe assistant error transcript entry for returned agent errors, even when the runtime emitted only an internal error stub.
- What changed: Runner failure payloads are marked `isError`, Gateway appends a deduped `<runId>:assistant-error` transcript entry for returned agent errors, and regression coverage now locks the returned-error, runtime-stub, and duplicate/idempotency paths.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No provider behavior changes, no delivery routing changes, no config/lifecycle changes, no raw transcript writes, and no dependency or lockfile changes.

## Motivation

- A user should not lose the visible assistant error after refreshing the Control UI. If a started run fails before normal assistant output, the live error event and durable history should agree.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #89197
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: A real Gateway/WebSocket Control UI run that fails at provider transport now leaves exactly one assistant error in refreshed `chat.history`.
- Real environment tested: Disposable Linux OpenClaw checkout with the patched PR branch, real Gateway server startup, real WebSocket RPC client, channels skipped with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, and an intentionally unreachable OpenAI-compatible base URL to trigger the provider failure without secrets.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts -- -t 'assistant error|returned agent-run error payloads|runtime emitted an internal error stub'
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.error-broadcast.test.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -- -t 'Agent failed before reply|assistant error|fallback candidate|error stub'
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node --import tsx ../real-chat-history-proof.ts after-gateway-force-v3
/usr/bin/time -v node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
Real runtime proof:
- provider transport failed with fetch failed / Connection error
- agent.wait status: error
- immediateHistoryCount: 3
- refreshHistoryCount: 3
- immediateAssistantCount: 1
- refreshAssistantCount: 1
- chatErrorEventCount: 1
- result: ONE_ASSISTANT_ERROR_IN_REFRESHED_HISTORY

Focused Gateway proof: 3 passed, 102 skipped.
Adjacent Gateway proof: 107 passed.
Session tool result guard proof: 35 passed.
Runner/Followup focused proof: 8 passed, 214 skipped.
git diff --check: clean.
```

- Observed result after fix: The live provider failure still broadcasts the expected chat error, and both immediate and post-reconnect `chat.history` contain one assistant error text: `Agent failed before reply: LLM request failed: network connection error.`
- What was not tested: Full repository-wide test suite, successful `tsgo` core on this testserver, real provider credentials, and a browser screenshot.
- Before evidence:

```text
Unpatched main proof for the same real Gateway/WebSocket/provider-failure path:
- sendStatus: started
- agent.wait status: ok
- immediateHistoryCount: 2
- refreshHistoryCount: 2
- immediateAssistantCount: 0
- refreshAssistantCount: 0
- chatErrorEventCount: 0
- result: ASSISTANT_ERROR_HISTORY_MISMATCH
```

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Runner final failure replies were not consistently marked as error payloads, and Gateway treated the runtime assistant-error callback as if it guaranteed a Control UI-visible `chat.history` assistant turn.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Previous coverage asserted live error broadcast behavior but did not prove refreshed `chat.history` retained an assistant error after a started real runtime failure.
- Contributing context (if known): Runtime/session guard error stubs can be internal transcript-only markers; Gateway still needs to durably mirror returned terminal agent errors into the Control UI history path.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`, and the temporary real Gateway/WebSocket proof harness.
- Scenario the test should lock in: A started run returns an agent error; Gateway appends one assistant error transcript entry, keeps the live error terminal event, survives reconnect/history refresh, and does not duplicate the assistant error.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The source tests cover the exact Gateway and runner seams, while the real proof exercises server startup, WebSocket RPC, provider transport failure, `agent.wait`, and refreshed `chat.history` in one flow.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing broadcast tests covered visible error delivery but not durable assistant transcript history.
- If no new test is added, why not: New regression assertions were added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Control UI users should now see the same failed assistant turn after refresh that they saw live when a started agent run fails before normal assistant output.

## Diagram

```text
real provider/runtime failure
  -> runner returns final failure payload marked isError
  -> Gateway returnedAgentErrorPayloads branch
  -> append one sanitized assistant error transcript entry with <runId>:assistant-error
  -> broadcast existing chat error terminal event
  -> reconnect chat.history still includes one assistant error
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No.
- Data access scope changed? No new scope; the change writes a sanitized assistant error to the same session transcript path already used for Gateway assistant persistence.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Not applicable.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux disposable test checkout
- Runtime/container: OpenClaw repository scripts with installed dependencies
- Model/provider: OpenAI-compatible provider configured to an unreachable local base URL for deterministic transport failure; no real credentials used
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI-style Gateway WebSocket RPC; external channels skipped with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`
- Relevant config (redacted): Dummy redacted API key, local unreachable base URL, temporary state directory

### Steps

1. Start the real Gateway server from the patched checkout.
2. Connect a WebSocket client and send `chat.send` for a Control UI session.
3. Trigger a deterministic provider transport failure through the unreachable model base URL.
4. Wait for the run with `agent.wait`.
5. Read `chat.history` immediately.
6. Reconnect a fresh WebSocket client and read `chat.history` again.
7. Count assistant text messages and chat error events.

### Expected

- The run surfaces a live terminal error.
- Refreshed `chat.history` contains exactly one assistant error message for the failed run.
- The assistant error is not duplicated.

### Actual

- Before: refreshed `chat.history` had zero assistant messages for the failed run.
- After: refreshed `chat.history` had exactly one assistant error message and one chat error event.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

```text
Before real proof result: ASSISTANT_ERROR_HISTORY_MISMATCH
After real proof result: ONE_ASSISTANT_ERROR_IN_REFRESHED_HISTORY
After source proof: Gateway focused 3/3, Gateway adjacent 107/107, session guard 35/35, Runner/Followup focused 8/8.
TypeScript core check on the testserver did not produce diagnostics; the process was terminated by signal 9 at about 1.78 GB RSS, so this is reported as a testserver resource blocker rather than a green typecheck claim.
```

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: Returned agent-run error payload detection, runner final failure `isError` marking, Gateway assistant-error transcript append, runtime internal error-stub callback interaction, idempotency key behavior, real provider transport failure, immediate history, refreshed history after reconnect, and existing chat error event behavior.
- Edge cases checked: Runtime internal error stub does not suppress the Control UI-visible returned error transcript; Gateway writes one `<runId>:assistant-error` entry for the returned terminal error; no dependency or lockfile files changed.
- What you did **not** verify: Full repo-wide suite, successful `tsgo` core on the resource-limited testserver, real external provider credentials, screenshot/recording, and production channels.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No.
- Migration needed? No.
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: Not applicable.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: A runtime-internal error stub could suppress the Control UI-visible returned error transcript.
  - Mitigation: Returned terminal agent errors now force the Gateway assistant-error transcript append while retaining the existing idempotency key.
- Risk: Gateway could duplicate assistant errors.
  - Mitigation: The transcript append uses a stable `<runId>:assistant-error` idempotency key and the source tests cover the one-message behavior.
- Risk: Error text could bypass display sanitization.
  - Mitigation: The Gateway fallback still uses `sanitizeAssistantDisplayText` before transcript append.
- Risk: Dependency Guard could require security approval.
  - Mitigation: No dependency manifests, lockfiles, or dependency blocks were changed.

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- Observed result after fix: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- What was not tested: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`
